Strictly weaker demand than `irrational_coeff_series_of_weighted_coeff_carry_block_certificates`: the supply problem shrinks from hitting one residue class mod `b^K` to hitting a low band of ≈ `b^L/q` residues.

declarations.name: irrational_coeff_series_of_carry_via_low_carry
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_coeff_series_of_carry_via_low_carry (b : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (b ^ K ∣ ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1))
declarations.docstring: **The low-carry interface subsumes the exact-carry interface**: the wave-14 carry-aware closure, re-derived through the residue-band engine. Machine proof that demanding exact first-block integrality (`b^K ∣ carrySum`) was overconstraint — a zero residue satisfies the residue-band height inequality verbatim.

declarations.name: irrational_tsum_totient_div_pow_two_of_totient_low_carry_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_tsum_totient_div_pow_two_of_totient_low_carry_certificates (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K * 2 ^ (L - K) + C + (N + L + 2)) < 2 ^ L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 low-carry socket.** Like the wave-14 carry-aware socket, but the first totient block may leave a residue: with `V = (∑_{r≤K} φ(N+r)·2^(K-r)) % 2^K`, certificates need only `q·(V·2^(L-K) + C + (N+L+2)) < 2^L`. This is the sharpened live route for #249 — the supply question becomes an interval-hit / low-band statement for the carry residue rather than an exact congruence. Certificate SUPPLY is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: irrational_tsum_totient_div_pow_two_of_totient_low_carry_full_block_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_tsum_totient_div_pow_two_of_totient_low_carry_full_block_certificates (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K : ℕ, q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K + (N + K + 2)) < 2 ^ K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 low-carry supply target, full-block form.** The `L = K, C = 0` specialisation: `∑_{n≥1} φ(n)/2^n` is irrational provided that for every `q` some window `(N, K)` has small carry residue, `q·((∑_{r≤K} φ(N+r)·2^(K-r)) % 2^K + (N+K+2)) < 2^K`. This is the exact on-disk form of the OPEN interval-hit obligation: a band of ≈ `2^K/q` acceptable residues at height `N ≲ 2^K/q`, against wave-14's single exact residue `0`. SUPPLY is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: totient_carry_sum_eq_range
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_sum_eq_range (M K : ℕ) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (M + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range K, Nat.totient (M + 1 + i) * 2 ^ (K - 1 - i)
declarations.docstring: **Icc→range normal form for the totient carry sum.** The first-block weighted sum re-indexed to `Finset.range`, the shape shared by the carry recurrences and by concrete certificate evaluation.

declarations.name: totient_carry_sum_succ_window
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_sum_succ_window (N K : ℕ) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (K + 1), Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K + 1 - r)) = 2 * (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) + Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Carry-field K-step (window growth), exact form.** Extending the window by one slot doubles the carry sum and feeds in the next totient: `T_{K+1}(N) = 2·T_K(N) + φ(N+K+1)`.

declarations.name: totient_carry_sum_bottom_split
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_sum_bottom_split (N K : ℕ) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (K + 1), Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K + 1 - r)) = 2 ^ K * Nat.totient (N + 1) + ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + 1 + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)
declarations.docstring: **Carry-field bottom split, exact form.** The `(K+1)`-window splits at its first slot: `T_{K+1}(N) = 2^K·φ(N+1) + T_K(N+1)`. Together with the K-step this yields the N-step recurrence.

declarations.name: totient_carry_sum_succ_shift
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_sum_succ_shift (N K : ℕ) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + 1 + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) + 2 ^ K * Nat.totient (N + 1) = 2 * (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) + Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Carry-field N-step (shift), exact form.** Shifting the window start by one obeys `T_K(N+1) + 2^K·φ(N+1) = 2·T_K(N) + φ(N+K+1)` — the exact integer form of the scout recurrence `V(N+1) = (2V(N) + φ(N+K+1)) mod 2^K`.

declarations.name: totient_carry_residue_succ_shift
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_residue_succ_shift (N K : ℕ) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + 1 + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K = (2 * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K) + Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))) % 2 ^ K
declarations.docstring: **Carry-residue N-step recurrence.** The residue field advances by an affine doubling map with totient input: `V_K(N+1) = (2·V_K(N) + φ(N+K+1)) mod 2^K`.

declarations.name: totient_carry_residue_succ_window
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_residue_succ_window (N K : ℕ) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (K + 1), Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K + 1 - r)) % 2 ^ (K + 1) = (2 * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K) + Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))) % 2 ^ (K + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Carry-residue K-step recurrence.** The same affine doubling map also advances precision: `V_{K+1}(N) = (2·V_K(N) + φ(N+K+1)) mod 2^{K+1}`. The K-climb `W_K = V_K/2^K` increases by `φ(N+K+1)/2^{K+1}` mod 1, so a supply hit is exactly a carry-out event of this climb.

declarations.name: totient_carry_residue_top_band_step
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_residue_top_band_step (N K : ℕ) (hf : Nat.totient (N + (K + 1)) < 2 ^ (K + 1)) (htop : 2 ^ (K + 1) ≤ 2 * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K) + Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))) : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (K + 1), Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K + 1 - r)) % 2 ^ (K + 1) < Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Top-band transfer (carry-out event).** If the residue sits within `φ(N+K+1)` of the top of its scale — `2^{K+1} ≤ 2·V_K(N) + φ(N+K+1)` — then one K-step wraps and the next residue is tiny: `V_{K+1}(N) < φ(N+K+1)`. Supply hits are exactly these carry-out events, so the open low-band obligation may equivalently be hunted at the top of the band, where the target width is `φ/2 ≈ N` instead of `2^K/q`.

declarations.name: totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_of_top_band
declarations.signature: theorem totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_of_top_band (q N K : ℕ) (hf : Nat.totient (N + (K + 1)) < 2 ^ (K + 1)) (htop : 2 ^ (K + 1) ≤ 2 * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K) + Nat.totient (N + (K + 1))) (hheight : q * (Nat.totient (N + (K + 1)) + (N + (K + 1) + 2)) ≤ 2 ^ (K + 1)) : q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (K + 1), Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K + 1 - r)) % 2 ^ (K + 1) + (N + (K + 1) + 2)) < 2 ^ (K + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Carry-out events supply certificates.** A top-band hit at scale `K` plus the height room `q·(φ(N+K+1) + N+(K+1)+2) ≤ 2^{K+1}` yields the full low-carry certificate at scale `K+1`. This is the machine-checked change of coordinates on the open supply problem: find one carry-out event with height room, and #249's certificate at that `q` exists.

declarations.name: totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_mono
declarations.signature: theorem totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_mono (q d N K : ℕ) (hle : d ≤ q) (hcert : q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K + (N + K + 2)) < 2 ^ K) : d * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K + (N + K + 2)) < 2 ^ K
declarations.docstring: **Certificate transport down the denominator ladder.** A low-carry full-block certificate at `q` is one at every `d ≤ q` with the same witness `(N, K)`.

declarations.name: coeff_series_shifted_ne_int_div_of_low_carry_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem coeff_series_shifted_ne_int_div_of_low_carry_certificate (b q N K L C : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hmiddle : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) (hpos : ∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) (harith : q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) % b ^ K * b ^ (L - K) + C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∀ a : ℤ, (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Per-`q` denominator exclusion, generic engine.** One low-carry certificate at a single `q` already denies every rational representation with denominator exactly `q`: the near-integer witness puts `q·(b^N·Σ − z)` strictly between consecutive integers, while `Σ = a/q` would make it an integer.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate (q N K : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hcert : q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K + (N + K + 2)) < 2 ^ K) : ∀ a : ℤ, (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 per-`q` denominator exclusion.** A single low-carry full-block certificate at `q` denies every representation `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n = a/q`. This is the per-precision slice of the open irrationality: instances are machine-checkable, the ∀q supply is not claimed.

declarations.name: totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_4838
declarations.signature: theorem totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_4838 : 4838 * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 34, Nat.totient (763 + r) * 2 ^ (34 - r)) % 2 ^ 34 + (763 + 34 + 2)) < 2 ^ 34
declarations.docstring: **Concrete machine-checked low-carry certificate at `q = 4838`** (witness `N = 763`, `K = 34`, residue `V = 3550228`): `4838·(3550228 + 763 + 34 + 2) = 17179868626 < 2^34`. Each window totient is certified by its prime factorization and rewritten to a literal before any arithmetic runs, so the proof is kernel-shallow and uses no compiler-trusted reflection.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_4838
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_4838 : ∀ (a : ℤ) (d : ℕ), 0 < d → d ≤ 4838 → (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (d : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `4838`.** Machine-checked: `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is not `a/d` for any integer `a` and any denominator `1 ≤ d ≤ 4838`; one certificate transports down the ladder via `totient_low_carry_full_block_certificate_mono`. Unconditional instance slice of #249; the ∀q supply (full irrationality) is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_4838
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_4838 : ∀ p : ℚ, p.den ≤ 4838 → (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (p : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion, `ℚ` form.** If `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is rational, its reduced denominator exceeds `4838`.

declarations.name: gap_band_sandwich
declarations.signature: private lemma gap_band_sandwich (bL qR UR WR jR s CR DR : ℝ) (hbL : 0 < bL) (hq : 0 < qR) (hdm : bL * jR + WR = qR * UR) (hW : 0 ≤ WR) (hs : 0 < s) (hsle : s ≤ CR / bL + DR / bL) (hband : WR + qR * (CR + DR) < bL) : jR < qR * (UR / bL + s) ∧ qR * (UR / bL + s) < jR + 1
declarations.docstring: **Real sandwich for the gap band.** Abstract core of the gap witness: with `bL·j + W = q·U` (Euclidean split of `q·U`), positive remainder `s` bounded by `(C + D)/bL`, and the band inequality `W + q·(C+D) < bL`, the scaled value `q·(U/bL + s)` lies strictly between `j` and `j + 1`.

declarations.name: strict_between_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_gap_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem strict_between_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_gap_certificate (b q N K L C : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hmiddle : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) (hpos : ∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) (harith : (q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) % b ^ K * b ^ (L - K))) % b ^ L + q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∃ z : ℤ, ∃ j : ℕ, (j : ℝ) < (q : ℝ) * ((b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)) ∧ (q : ℝ) * ((b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)) < (j : ℝ) + 1
declarations.docstring: **Gap witness.** Under the gap certificate `(q·(V·b^(L−K))) % b^L + q·(C + (N+L+2)) < b^L` (with `V` the K-window residue), the shifted series lands strictly inside a consecutive-integer gap after scaling by `q`: `j < q·(b^N·Σ − z) < j + 1` for the explicit `j = (q·V·b^(L−K)) / b^L`.

declarations.name: coeff_series_shifted_ne_int_div_of_gap_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem coeff_series_shifted_ne_int_div_of_gap_certificate (b q N K L C : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hmiddle : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) (hpos : ∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) (harith : (q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) % b ^ K * b ^ (L - K))) % b ^ L + q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∀ a : ℤ, (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Per-`q` denominator exclusion from a gap certificate, generic engine.** A gap certificate at `q` denies every rational representation with denominator exactly `q`: the witness puts `q·(b^N·Σ − z)` strictly between the consecutive integers `j` and `j+1`, while `Σ = a/q` would make it an integer. The landed low-carry (bottom-band) certificate is the `j = 0` special case.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_totient_gap_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_totient_gap_certificate (q N K : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hcert : (q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K)) % 2 ^ K + q * (N + K + 2) < 2 ^ K) : ∀ a : ℤ, (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 per-`q` denominator exclusion from a gap certificate.** The gap certificate `(q·V_K(N)) mod 2^K + q·(N+K+2) < 2^K` — satisfiable by a `1 − O(q·N/2^K)` fraction of residues instead of the bottom band's `O(1/q)` fraction — denies every representation `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n = a/q`.

declarations.name: totient_gap_certificate_of_low_carry_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem totient_gap_certificate_of_low_carry_certificate (q N K : ℕ) (hcert : q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K + (N + K + 2)) < 2 ^ K) : (q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K)) % 2 ^ K + q * (N + K + 2) < 2 ^ K
declarations.docstring: **Bottom-band certificates transport into the gap engine.** Every landed low-carry certificate is a gap certificate (the `j = 0` band), so the gap engine strictly supersedes the wave-16 engine.

declarations.name: irrational_tsum_totient_div_pow_two_of_gap_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_tsum_totient_div_pow_two_of_gap_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K : ℕ, (q * ((∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, Nat.totient (N + r) * 2 ^ (K - r)) % 2 ^ K)) % 2 ^ K + q * (N + K + 2) < 2 ^ K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Gap-certificate supply closes Erdős #249.** If every denominator `q` admits one window `(N, K)` whose residue avoids the thin top band — width `q·(N+K+2)` out of `2^K`, versus the bottom band's required width — then `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is irrational. This is the (strictly weaker) remaining open obligation in gap coordinates; it is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: totient_gap_row_window_1_120
declarations.docstring: Single gap-certificate row at the wave-17 window `(N,K) = (1,120)`: `(lo·V) mod 2^120 + lo·123 < 2^120` as a kernel-computable boolean.

declarations.name: totient_carry_residue_window_1_120_eq
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carry_residue_window_1_120_eq : (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 120, Nat.totient (1 + r) * 2 ^ (120 - r)) % 2 ^ 120 = 977330308222705062435708544085600126
declarations.docstring: **Wave-17 window residue, kernel-evaluated.** The `K = 120` carry residue at shift `N = 1`: every window totient (all ≤ 112) is certified by its prime factorization and rewritten to a literal before arithmetic.

declarations.name: totient_carry_residue_window_1_240_eq
declarations.docstring: **Window residue `(N,K) = (1,240)`, kernel-evaluated.** Every window totient is certified by its prime factorization and rewritten to a literal before arithmetic — no `Nat.totient` kernel unfolding at any point.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_248672326362367909
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_248672326362367909 : ∀ (a : ℤ) (d : ℕ), 0 < d → d ≤ 248672326362367909 → (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (d : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `248672326362367909`.** Machine-checked: `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is not `a/d` for any integer `a` and any denominator `1 ≤ d ≤ 248672326362367909`. The Farey-gap feed (`GapFareyBound.gap_check_window_1_120_le_248672326362367909`) replaces bulk kernel enumeration; every denominator receives its certificate from the single window `(N, K) = (1, 120)`. Unconditional instance slice of #249; the ∀q supply is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_248672326362367909
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_248672326362367909 : ∀ p : ℚ, p.den ≤ 248672326362367909 → (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (p : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `248672326362367909`, `ℚ` form.** If `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is rational, its reduced denominator exceeds `248672326362367909`.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_4194304
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_4194304 : ∀ (a : ℤ) (d : ℕ), 0 < d → d ≤ 4194304 → (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (d : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `4194304` — retained name.** Now a one-line corollary of the Farey-gap bound; the depth-22 bulk `decide +kernel` enumeration this name used to carry is superseded (same statement, strictly cheaper proof, identical trusted base).

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_4194304
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_4194304 : ∀ p : ℚ, p.den ≤ 4194304 → (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (p : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `4194304`, `ℚ` form.** If `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is rational, its reduced denominator exceeds `4194304`.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_int_div_of_den_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `79639646646701375323355774875831053`.** Machine-checked: `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is not `a/d` for any integer `a` and any denominator `1 ≤ d ≤ 79639646646701375323355774875831053`. The Farey-gap feed (`GapFareyBound.gap_check_window_1_240_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053`) replaces bulk kernel enumeration; every denominator receives its certificate from the single window `(N, K) = (1, 240)`. Unconditional instance slice of #249; the ∀q supply is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_ne_ratCast_of_den_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #249 denominator exclusion to `79639646646701375323355774875831053`, `ℚ` form.** If `∑_{n≥0} φ(n)/2^n` is rational, its reduced denominator exceeds `79639646646701375323355774875831053`.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_pnat_half_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_pnat_half_pow : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Index bridge**: the #249 statement-shape series over `ℕ` equals the ladder-convention `ℕ+` series with explicit `(1/2)ⁿ` terms.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(φ) = 2`**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2^d-1) = 2`, exactly. The Lambert transform of the #249 coefficients at the #249 point is rational — the difficulty of #249 lives entirely on the power-series side.

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_half
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_half : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 1 / 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(μ) = 1/2`**: `∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d-1) = 1/2`, exactly.

declarations.name: tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_totient_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_totient_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((MersenneLambertLadder.primWeight (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = ∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n
declarations.docstring: **The positive lift `L(A) = S`**: the #249 series IS the Mersenne–Lambert transform of the nonnegative primitive-conductor weight `A = φ * μ` (`A(p) = p - 2`, `A(p^e) = (p-1)²·p^(e-2)`, `A ≥ 0`, `MersenneLambertLadder.primWeight`). Same shape as the Erdős–Borwein constant `∑ 1/(2^d-1)` — whose irrationality IS machine-checked in this kernel — with weight `1` replaced by `A`.

declarations.name: totient_series_eq_half_add_moebius_mersenne_square
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_eq_half_add_moebius_mersenne_square : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens**: `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ = 1/2 + ∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d-1)²`. The #249 constant as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series.

declarations.name: totient_series_eq_half_add_mobius_mersenne_square
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_eq_half_add_mobius_mersenne_square : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: Capped socket-name alias (`cap_quick_erdos_249_analytic_route_candidate_mobiu_0916cee1cba1`).

declarations.name: tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one_ne_int_div_of_den_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one_ne_int_div_of_den_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053 : ∀ (a : ℤ) (d : ℕ), 0 < d → d ≤ 79639646646701375323355774875831053 → (∑' k : ℕ+, ((MersenneLambertLadder.primWeight (k : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (k : ℕ) - 1)) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (d : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Record-bound transfer to the positive lift.** The primitive-conductor Erdős–Borwein series `∑ A(d)/(2^d-1)` has no rational representation with denominator `≤ 79639646646701375323355774875831053` — the wave-17.5 Farey record, restated on the ladder's positive representation of `S`.

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_sq_ne_int_div_of_den_le_39819823323350687661677887437915526
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_sq_ne_int_div_of_den_le_39819823323350687661677887437915526 : ∀ (a : ℤ) (d : ℕ), 0 < d → d ≤ 39819823323350687661677887437915526 → (∑' k : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (k : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (k : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2) ≠ (a : ℝ) / (d : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Record-bound transfer to the Möbius-square lens.** The signed series `T = ∑ μ(d)/(2^d-1)²` has no rational representation with denominator `≤ 39819823323350687661677887437915526` (half the wave-17.5 record): `T = a/d` would force `S = (d+2a)/(2d)` with `2d` under the record bound.

declarations.name: closes
declarations.signature: theorem closes the picture: `∑_{g≥1} P(gcd(X,Y) = g) = 1` exactly — the probabilistic content of the rational rung `L(φ) = 2` — and the wave-17.5 Farey record transfers: `P(gcd(X,Y) = 1)` has no rational representation with denominator `≤ 3.98×10³⁴`. No irrationality of `S` is claimed; the coordinate change relocates #249 onto the visible lattice, it does not solve it. -/ /-- **Index bridge, `ℕ`-power form**: the #249 statement-shape series with explicit `(1/2)ⁿ` terms, still over `ℕ`. -/ theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_nat_half_pow : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_nat_half_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_nat_half_pow : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n
declarations.docstring: **Index bridge, `ℕ`-power form**: the #249 statement-shape series with explicit `(1/2)ⁿ` terms, still over `ℕ`.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_pairs_eq_totient_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_visible_coprime_pairs_eq_totient_series : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n
declarations.docstring: **The #249 constant IS a visible-lattice mass**: summing `2^{-(a+b)}` over the half-open coprime pairs (`a ≥ 1`, `b ≥ 0`, `gcd(a,b) = 1`) gives exactly `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ` — no boundary correction, because the visible-point count on the half-open antidiagonal equals `φ(n)` for every `n` including `0` and `1`.

declarations.name: totient_series_eq_half_add_visible_coprime_pairs
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_eq_half_add_visible_coprime_pairs : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = 1 / 2 + ∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, (if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0)
declarations.docstring: **Fair-coin geometric coprimality**: `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ = 1/2 + P(gcd(X,Y) = 1)`, where the probability is spelled as its defining series `∑_{(a,b) coprime, a,b ≥ 1} 2^{-(a+b)}`. The boundary term `1/2` is `P(X = 1) = L(μ)` — the ladder's Möbius rung. Erdős #249 is equivalent to irrationality of this coprimality probability.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_pairs_ne_int_div_of_den_le_39819823323350687661677887437915526
declarations.docstring: **Record-bound transfer to the coprimality probability.** The probability that two independent fair-coin waiting times are coprime, `P(gcd(X,Y) = 1) = ∑_{(a,b)=1} 2^{-(a+b)}`, has no rational representation with denominator `≤ 39819823323350687661677887437915526` (half the wave-17.5 Farey record): `P = a/d` would force `S = (d+2a)/(2d)` with `2d` under the record bound.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_gcd_layers_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_visible_coprime_gcd_layers_eq_one : ∑' g : ℕ, (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (g + 1)) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The fair-coin gcd law is exactly normalized**: layer `g` of the sum is `P(gcd(X,Y) = g+1)` — the coprime-pair mass at scale `(1/2)^(g+1)`, by the unique factorization `(a,b) = g·(a',b')` — and the layers sum to `1`: the gcd of two independent fair-coin waiting times is finite almost surely. Through the visible-coprime bridge this is the probabilistic content of the wave-18 rational rung `L(φ) = 2`. The open #249 value `S - 1/2` is the `g = 1` atom of this exactly-normalized distribution.

declarations.name: tsum_inv_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_inv_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ).divisors.card : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **E[τ(gcd)] — the q-zeta anchor of the level-2 ladder**: `∑_{d≥1} 1/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (σ(n) - τ(n))·(1/2)ⁿ`, exactly, with the coefficient spelled as its divisor sums. This is `ζ_q(2) - ζ_q(1)` at `q = 1/2`; its irrationality (q-Padé) is cited, not formalised.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **E[gcd(X,Y)] — the first moment of the fair-coin gcd law**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (P(n) - n)·(1/2)ⁿ`, where `P(n) = ∑_{e∣n} φ(e)·(n/e)` is Pillai's gcd-sum function.

declarations.name: tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ d ∣ p.1 ∧ d ∣ p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **The divisibility factorization** — foundation stone of the squared kernel: the fair-coin mass of `{(a,b) : a,b ≥ 1, d ∣ a, d ∣ b}` is exactly `1/(2ᵈ-1)²`, i.e. `P(d ∣ gcd(X,Y)) = P(d ∣ X)·P(d ∣ Y)`.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_visible_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (p.1 + p.2) - 1) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The reduced-direction law**: the Mersenne masses of the reduced slopes sum to one — `∑_{(a,b) coprime, a,b ≥ 1} 1/(2^{a+b}-1) = 1`. This is the root cylinder of the Stern–Brocot law, proved through the wave-19 gcd-layer normalization.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_cylinder_mass_error_le
declarations.signature: theorem stern_brocot_cylinder_mass_error_le (dp : ℕ) (a b : ℕ+) : GcdMomentCalculus.sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ≤ 1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1)) ∧ 1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1)) - GcdMomentCalculus.sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ≤ (2 / 3 : ℝ) ^ dp * (1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1)))
declarations.docstring: **Stern–Brocot cylinder approximation with explicit rate**: the depth-`d` unfolding of the mediant recursion at `(a,b)` under-approximates the closed form `1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1))` with error at most `(2/3)ᵈ` of it.

declarations.name: tendsto_stern_brocot_cylinder_mass
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_stern_brocot_cylinder_mass (a b : ℕ+) : Filter.Tendsto (fun dp : ℕ => GcdMomentCalculus.sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b) Filter.atTop (nhds (1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1))))
declarations.docstring: **The Stern–Brocot cylinder law, limit form**: the mediant recursion's depth-`d` masses converge to `M(a,b) = 1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1)) = P(a∣X)·P(b∣Y)`. At the root, `M(1,1) = 1` — the reduced-direction law.

declarations.name: two_pow_mul_totient_series_eq_prefix_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem two_pow_mul_totient_series_eq_prefix_add_tail (N : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientPrefix N : ℝ) + TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N
declarations.docstring: **Shift identity**: `2^N·S = Φ_N + R_N` — the local totient tail is the fractional layer of `2^N·S`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_certified_kill
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_certified_kill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h N L) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate soundness**: a residue of the window discrepancy `A_{h,N,L}` modulo `2^L` outside the radius-`(N+h+L+2)` neighbourhood of `0` kills period `h` at position `N` — the tail difference is provably not an integer.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_eventual_tail_period
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_eventual_tail_period (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)) : ∃ h : ℕ, 0 < h ∧ ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ N, N₀ ≤ N → TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Tail-period law**: if `S` is rational then some period `h ≥ 1` makes every shifted tail difference an integer from the pre-period `v₂(den)` on.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-21 reduction (the throat of #249)**: a certified period-kill supply — every period `h ≥ 1` killed beyond every threshold — proves `S` irrational.

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_killed_upto_eight
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_killed_upto_eight : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h 12 16
declarations.docstring: **Concrete deposit**: every period `h ≤ 8` is certified dead at `(N, L) = (12, 16)` — machine-checked residue certificates.

declarations.name: totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne (r : ℚ) (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h8 : h ≤ 8) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ 12 * (2 ^ h - 1)) : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (r : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Concrete denominator exclusion**: `S` is not a rational whose denominator divides `2¹²·(2ʰ-1)` for any `1 ≤ h ≤ 8` — no dyadic rational up to `2¹²`, and no odd part whose multiplicative order of `2` is at most `8`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_carry_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_carry_recurrence (M : ℕ) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (M + 1) = 2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail M - (Nat.totient (M + 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Carry recurrence**: `R_{M+1} = 2·R_M - φ(M+1)` — doubling the tail shifts the window by one and expels the head totient.

declarations.name: totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_survivor_kill
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_survivor_kill {h N K : ℕ} (hkill : TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill h N K) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Survivor soundness**: the decidable carry-survivor certificate — every integer candidate in the initial box escapes the strip `|·| < N+i+h+2` within `K` steps — kills period `h` at position `N`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_diff_multiple_telescope
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_diff_multiple_telescope (h N m : ℕ) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + m * h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range m, (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + i * h + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + i * h))
declarations.docstring: **Period-ray telescoping**: the `m·h` tail difference is the sum of `m` translated `h` tail differences — integrality flows down every multiple.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ h₀ : ℕ, 0 < h₀ → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ m, 0 < m ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ K, TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill (m * h₀) N K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-22 reduction**: a survivor kill along ANY multiple of each primitive period, at arbitrarily large `N`, proves `S` irrational — the weakest supply hypothesis in the ladder.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ h₀ : ℕ, 0 < h₀ → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ m, 0 < m ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (m * h₀) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The endpoint-window variant of the multiple-period reduction.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ K, TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) N K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **One-parameter collapse**: survivor kills along the single family `lcm(1..t)` at arbitrarily large `t` and `N` prove #249.

declarations.name: totient_tail_survivor_strict_at_equal_budget
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_survivor_strict_at_equal_budget : TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill 1 57 8 ∧ ∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill 1 57 L
declarations.docstring: **Strictness witness**: at `(h, N) = (1, 57)` the survivor certificate kills at depth `8` while every endpoint window `L ≤ 8` fails — the survivor predicate is strictly stronger at equal budget (and measured near-equivalent in minimal depth by the wave-22 probe).

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_killed_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_killed_upto_sixteen : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 16, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h 14 9
declarations.docstring: **Concrete deposit**: every period `h ≤ 16` is certified dead at `(N, L) = (14, 9)` — twice the wave-21 period range in a smaller window.

declarations.name: totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne_upto_sixteen (r : ℚ) (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h16 : h ≤ 16) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ 14 * (2 ^ h - 1)) : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (r : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Extended denominator exclusion**: `S` is not a rational whose denominator divides `2¹⁴·(2ʰ-1)` for any `1 ≤ h ≤ 16` — subsumes the wave-21 lattice on both axes.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Endpoint lcm collapse (general position)**: certified window kills along `lcm(1..t)` at arbitrarily large `t` and `N` prove #249 — fills the endpoint gap of the wave-22 survivor-only lcm reduction.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-23 diagonal collapse**: #249 follows from ONE ℕ-indexed sequence of decidable statements — the endpoint certificate firing on the diagonal `(h, N) = (lcm(1..t), lcm(1..t))` for infinitely many `t`. No second parameter remains.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ K, TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Survivor variant of the diagonal collapse.

declarations.name: period_lcm_window_index_prime_pow_of_not_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem period_lcm_window_index_prime_pow_of_not_dvd {t j : ℕ} (hj : 0 < j) (hlt : j < 2 * t) (hnd : ¬ j ∣ TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) : ∃ p k : ℕ, Nat.Prime p ∧ j = p ^ k ∧ t < j
declarations.docstring: **Window structure of the lcm ray**: below `2t` every non-divisor of `lcm(1..t)` is a bare prime power exceeding `t` — all other window indices divide the period.

declarations.name: totient_period_lcm_ray_factorisation
declarations.signature: theorem totient_period_lcm_ray_factorisation {t j : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hdvd : j ∣ TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (hclean : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t / j)) : Nat.totient (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + j) = Nat.totient j * Nat.totient (q * (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t / j) + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Lcm-ray window factorisation**: on a clean divisor `j` of `lcm(1..t)` the window totient splits exactly into the known local part `φ(j)` and a cofactor totient whose argument is forced `≡ 1` mod every non-exhausted prime of `j`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_killed_upto_six
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_killed_upto_six : ∀ t ∈ Finset.Icc 1 6, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.diagonalKillDepth t)
declarations.docstring: **Diagonal deposits**: the wave-23 predicate `P t` is kernel-checked for every `t ≤ 6` at the probe-tabulated depths.

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_killed_at_seven_and_eight
declarations.docstring: `P 7` and `P 8`: diagonal kills at `lcm(1..7) = 420` (depth `14`) and `lcm(1..8) = 840` (depth `15`).

declarations.name: period_lcm_dvd_period_lcm_of_le
declarations.signature: theorem period_lcm_dvd_period_lcm_of_le {t u : ℕ} (htu : t ≤ u) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t ∣ TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm u
declarations.docstring: **Scale-ladder divisibility**: `lcm(1..t) ∣ lcm(1..u)` for `t ≤ u` — the cones at all scales form one directed system, so cross-scale cone pairs are same-scale pairs on the finer ray.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)) : ∃ t₁ : ℕ, ∀ t, t₁ ≤ t → ∀ q m : ℕ, 0 < q → TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + m * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-cone flatness law**: if the totient series is rational, then from some scale on the whole lcm cone is fractionally flat — every difference `R_{q·H_t + m·H_t} - R_{q·H_t}` is an integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (m * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-24 cone collapse**: certified window kills anywhere on the two-multiplier lcm cone — `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t` — prove #249. The wave-23 diagonal is the cell `q = m = 1`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_window_kill_exists_iff_tail_diff_not_int
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_window_kill_exists_iff_tail_diff_not_int (h N : ℕ) : (∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h N L) ↔ TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness**: some depth certifies `(h, N)` iff the tail difference `R_{N+h} - R_N` is a non-integer. Certificates are receipts; non-integrality is the object.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The diagonal frontier in pure form**: non-integral diagonal tail differences at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249 — no certificate vocabulary in the hypothesis.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + m * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone frontier in pure form**: one non-integral cone difference at arbitrarily large scale proves #249.

declarations.name: totient_tail_second_diff_not_int_of_rank_two_kill
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_second_diff_not_int_of_rank_two_kill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedRank2Kill h N L) : (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + 2 * h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h)) - (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 soundness**: a certified second-difference residue proves `(R_{N+2h} - R_{N+h}) - (R_{N+h} - R_N)` is not an integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank_two_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank_two_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedRank2Kill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 cone supply**: certified second-difference kills along the q-rays of the lcm cone at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249.

declarations.name: totient_tail_lcm_cone_cells_killed
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_lcm_cone_cells_killed : TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (3 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 6 ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) 7 ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (3 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) 8
declarations.docstring: **Cone-lattice deposits off the diagonal**: certified kills at `(t, q, m) = (2, 3, 1)`, `(3, 2, 2)`, `(3, 1, 3)` at the probe-tabulated depths.

declarations.name: totient_tail_rank_two_kill_sound_but_not_shallower_cell
declarations.docstring: **The measured rank-2 verdict, kernel-checked**: at `(h, N) = (1, 8)` the rank-1 certificate fires at depth `8`, no rank-2 certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8`, and rank-2 first fires at depth `9` — sound but not shallower (probe v2, `t ≤ 20`: rank-1 at least as shallow in 30 of 40 cells).

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_not_int_at_seven_and_eight
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_not_int_at_seven_and_eight : (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 7 + TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 7) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 7) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) ∧ (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 8 + TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 8) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 8) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The diagonal tail differences at `t = 7` and `t = 8` are non-integers — the wave-23 corollary extended past its decide ceiling.

declarations.name: totient_tail_nonintegral_pair_of_cone_nonflat_cert
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_nonintegral_pair_of_cone_nonflat_cert {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) : ∃ qi ∈ Q, ∃ qj ∈ Q, TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (qj * H) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (qi * H) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Menu refutation soundness**: a firing non-flatness certificate (with one-sided floors) forces some pair of menu vertices to have a non-integral tail difference.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, ∃ Q : List ℕ, Q ≠ [] ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, 0 < q) ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L Q) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone non-flatness supply theorem**: one firing vertex menu at arbitrarily large scale proves #249.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm (t + 1) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-jump supply theorem**: certified kills at the jumps `(h, N) = (H_{t+1} - H_t, H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249 — the cone cells `(q, m) = (1, p-1)`, with no side conditions at all.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cells_certified
declarations.docstring: First firing menus at the probe-tabulated smallest cells.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 8 [1, 3] ∧ (∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) L) ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 9
declarations.docstring: **The measured strict win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[1, 3]` refutes joint flatness at depth `8` while no pairwise certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8` (the pair first kills at depth `9`).

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3, 4] ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3]) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 4]) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [3, 4]) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2)
declarations.docstring: **The measured joint win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[2, 3, 4]` refutes joint flatness at depth `5` while every 2-vertex submenu refuter and every pairwise certificate is silent at depth `5` — the menu is inconsistent at a depth where every pair alone stays consistent.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_iff_no_common_point
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_iff_no_common_point {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q ↔ ¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Exactness of the menu refuter**: the certificate fires iff the menu arcs share NO common point — the probe's brute-force gate (`nonflat_matches_common_point_bruteforce`), now a theorem at every cell.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_common_point_halving_descent
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_common_point_halving_descent {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (h : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneCommonPoint H (L + 1) Q) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Halving descent**: a common point of the depth-`L+1` menu arcs yields one at depth `L` (floors held at `L`).

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_depth_monotone
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_depth_monotone {H L L' : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L' Q
declarations.docstring: **Depth monotonicity of the menu refuter**: a firing certificate keeps firing at every deeper depth (floors held at the base), so `nonflat_L_min` is THE firing threshold.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_supermenu
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_supermenu {H L : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q'
declarations.docstring: **Menu monotonicity**: a firing certificate keeps firing for every supermenu at the SAME depth — adding cone vertices cannot refill an empty arc intersection. This is why the wave-25 joint wins exist (firing runs upward in the menu) and why a small seed suffices.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_seed_lift
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_seed_lift {H L L' : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L' Q'
declarations.docstring: **Seed-lift**: a small seed menu firing at its own floor inflates to every supermenu at every deeper depth (depth monotonicity up, menu monotonicity out). For supply it suffices that some prefix seed fires at its floor for arbitrarily large `t`.

declarations.name: farey_gap
declarations.signature: theorem farey_gap {a b c d r s : ℤ} (hb : 0 < b) (hd : 0 < d) (hdet : b * c - a * d = 1) (hleft : a * s < r * b) (hright : r * d < c * s) : b + d ≤ s
declarations.docstring: **Mediant / Farey gap lemma.** If `a/b < r/s < c/d` (with `b, d > 0`) and the neighbours are unimodular (`b*c - a*d = 1`), then `b + d ≤ s`. Elementary: `s = b·(c·s - r·d) + d·(r·b - a·s)` and both bracketed factors are `≥ 1`.

declarations.name: det_one
declarations.docstring: Unimodularity of the two Farey neighbours: `b*c - a*d = 1`.

declarations.name: left_in
declarations.docstring: Left neighbour is strictly below the left endpoint `(V'-123)/2^120`: `a/b < (V'-123)/2^120`, i.e. `a * 2^120 < (V'-123) * b`.

declarations.name: right_in
declarations.docstring: Right endpoint `V'/2^120` is `≤` the right neighbour `c/d`: `V' * d ≤ c * 2^120`.

declarations.name: gap_check_window_1_120_le_248672326362367909
declarations.docstring: **The bound.** Every denominator `q` with `0 < q ≤ 248672326362367909` satisfies the wave-17 gap certificate for the `(N=1, K=120)` window.

declarations.name: det_one_240
declarations.docstring: Unimodularity of the `K = 240` Farey neighbours: `b*c - a*d = 1`.

declarations.name: left_in_240
declarations.docstring: Left neighbour is strictly below the left endpoint `(V'-243)/2^240`.

declarations.name: right_in_240
declarations.docstring: Right endpoint `V'/2^240` is `≤` the right neighbour `c/d`.

declarations.name: gap_check_window_1_240_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
declarations.docstring: **The `K = 240` bound.** Every denominator `q` with `0 < q ≤ 79639646646701375323355774875831053` satisfies the gap certificate for the `(N=1, K=240)` window.

declarations.name: tsum_pnat_pred_mul_geometric
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pnat_pred_mul_geometric {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : ∑' m : ℕ+, (((m : ℕ) : ℝ) - 1) * x ^ (m : ℕ) = (x / (1 - x)) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: `∑_{m≥1} (m-1)·xᵐ = (x/(1-x))²` — the pure square of the geometric tail, with the `m = 1` term vanishing. This is the second-weight profile that turns the wave-18 pair engine into the squared-kernel transfer.

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq_pure
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq_pure (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e * ((((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ) - 1)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed linear-growth PURE-squared-Lambert identity**: `∑_d w(d)·(rᵈ/(1-rᵈ))² = ∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e)·(n/e - 1))·rⁿ` for `|w(d)| ≤ d`. The divisor coefficient is `(w * Id)(n) - (w * ζ)(n)`: one theorem carries the whole level-2 ladder. Rides wave 18's `tsum_lambert_pair_regroup` with second weight `v(m) = m - 1`.

declarations.name: tsum_one_lambert_sq_pure
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_one_lambert_sq_pure {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ).divisors.card : ℝ)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **`L₂(1)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑_d (rᵈ/(1-rᵈ))² = ∑_n (σ(n) - τ(n))·rⁿ`. The coefficient is spelled as its divisor sums: `σ(n) = ∑_{e∣n} n/e` and `τ(n) = #divisors(n)`.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_lambert_sq_pure
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_lambert_sq_pure {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **`L₂(φ)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑_d φ(d)·(rᵈ/(1-rᵈ))² = ∑_n (P(n) - n)·rⁿ`, where `P(n) = ∑_{e∣n} φ(e)·(n/e)` is Pillai's gcd-sum function (`φ * Id`); the subtracted `n` is `φ * ζ = Id`.

declarations.name: half_pow_sq_term
declarations.signature: private lemma half_pow_sq_term' (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d)) ^ 2 = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2

declarations.name: tsum_one_div_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_one_div_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ).divisors.card : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The q-zeta anchor of the level-2 ladder**: `∑_{d≥1} 1/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (σ(n) - τ(n))·(1/2)ⁿ`, exactly. Probability reading: `E[τ(gcd(X,Y))] = ∑_d P(d ∣ gcd)`. In q-zeta terms this value is `ζ_q(2) - ζ_q(1)` at `q = 1/2` (Jouhet–Mosaki convention), irrational by the Postelmans–Van Assche q-Padé theorem — cited, NOT formalised; what is machine-checked here is the identity.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The first gcd moment**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (P(n) - n)·(1/2)ⁿ` with `P` Pillai's gcd-sum function. Probability reading: `E[gcd(X,Y)] = ∑_d φ(d)·P(d ∣ gcd)` — the expected gcd of two independent fair-coin waiting times, as a totient-weighted squared-Mersenne series.

declarations.name: tsum_pow_succ_geometric
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pow_succ_geometric' {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : ∑' g : ℕ, x ^ (g + 1) = x / (1 - x)
declarations.docstring: **The first gcd moment**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (P(n) - n)·(1/2)ⁿ` with `P` Pillai's gcd-sum function. Probability reading: `E[gcd(X,Y)] = ∑_d φ(d)·P(d ∣ gcd)` — the expected gcd of two independent fair-coin waiting times, as a totient-weighted squared-Mersenne series. -/ theorem tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ) := by have h := tsum_totient_lambert_sq_pure (r := 1 / 2) (by norm_num) (by norm_num) calc ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) * (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 := by refine tsum_congr fun d => ?_ rw [half_pow_sq_term' (d : ℕ) d.pos, mul_one_div] _ = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ

declarations.name: tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ d ∣ p.1 ∧ d ∣ p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **Divisibility of the pair factorizes**: the fair-coin mass of `{(a,b) : a,b ≥ 1, d ∣ a, d ∣ b}` is exactly `1/(2ᵈ-1)²` — i.e. `P(d ∣ gcd(X,Y)) = P(d ∣ X)·P(d ∣ Y)`. This is the foundation stone of the squared kernel: `L₂(f) = ∑ f(d)·P(d ∣ gcd)`, and it is also why the Stern–Brocot cylinder closed form `1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1))` is a product of one-variable divisor probabilities.

declarations.name: tsum_pos_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (p.1 + p.2) - 1) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The reduced-direction law**: `∑_{(a,b) coprime, a,b ≥ 1} 1/(2^{a+b}-1) = 1`. Every positive pair `(X,Y)` has a unique reduced slope, and `P(slope = a/b) = ∑_g 2^{-g(a+b)} = 1/(2^{a+b}-1)` for coprime `(a,b)`; the masses sum to one. Proved by expanding each Mersenne reciprocal as its geometric layer series and swapping with the wave-19 gcd-layer normalization. This is the root cylinder of the Stern–Brocot law: `M(1,1) = 1`.

declarations.name: cylinderMass
declarations.signature: noncomputable def cylinderMass (a b : ℕ+) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The closed-form cylinder mass at the coprime node `(a,b)`: `M(a,b) = 1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1)) = P(a ∣ X)·P(b ∣ Y)`.

declarations.name: sternBrocotDepthMass
declarations.signature: noncomputable def sternBrocotDepthMass : ℕ → ℕ+ → ℕ+ → ℝ | 0, _, _ => 0 | (dp + 1), a, b => 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ ((a : ℕ) + (b : ℕ)) - 1) + sternBrocotDepthMass dp (a + b) b + sternBrocotDepthMass dp a (a + b) private lemma two_le_two_pow (a : ℕ+) : (2 : ℝ) ≤ (2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: The depth-`d` finite unfolding of the mediant recursion: sum the stop mass `1/(2^{a+b}-1)` at every node of the first `d` generations of the subtree rooted at `(a,b)`, under the children `(a+b, b)` and `(a, a+b)`.

declarations.name: two_le_two_pow
declarations.signature: private lemma two_le_two_pow (a : ℕ+) : (2 : ℝ) ≤ (2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ)

declarations.name: cylinderMass_pos
declarations.signature: theorem cylinderMass_pos (a b : ℕ+) : 0 < cylinderMass a b

declarations.name: cylinderMass_split
declarations.signature: theorem cylinderMass_split (a b : ℕ+) : cylinderMass a b = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ ((a : ℕ) + (b : ℕ)) - 1) + cylinderMass (a + b) b + cylinderMass a (a + b)
declarations.docstring: **The exact telescoping recursion of the cylinder mass**: `M(a,b) = 1/(2^{a+b}-1) + M(a+b, b) + M(a, a+b)`. With `A = 2ᵃ`, `B = 2ᵇ` this is `1/((A-1)(B-1)) = 1/(AB-1) + 1/((AB-1)(B-1)) + 1/((A-1)(AB-1))` — the algebra that makes the reduced-direction distribution an exact rational Markov measure on the Stern–Brocot tree.

declarations.name: stop_ge_third_cylinderMass
declarations.signature: private lemma stop_ge_third_cylinderMass (a b : ℕ+) : (1 / 3) * cylinderMass a b ≤ 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ ((a : ℕ) + (b : ℕ)) - 1)
declarations.docstring: The stop mass at a node is at least a third of its cylinder mass: `(1/3)·M(a,b) ≤ 1/(2^{a+b}-1)`, i.e. `2^{a+b} - 1 ≤ 3(2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1)` — equality exactly at the root `(1,1)`.

declarations.name: cylinderMass_children_le
declarations.signature: theorem cylinderMass_children_le (a b : ℕ+) : cylinderMass (a + b) b + cylinderMass a (a + b) ≤ (2 / 3) * cylinderMass a b
declarations.docstring: The two children carry at most `2/3` of the parent cylinder mass.

declarations.name: sternBrocotDepthMass_error
declarations.signature: theorem sternBrocotDepthMass_error (dp : ℕ) : ∀ a b : ℕ+, 0 ≤ cylinderMass a b - sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ∧ cylinderMass a b - sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ≤ (2 / 3 : ℝ) ^ dp * cylinderMass a b
declarations.docstring: **Finite-depth cylinder approximation with explicit geometric rate**: `0 ≤ M(a,b) - M_d(a,b) ≤ (2/3)ᵈ·M(a,b)`. The depth-`d` unfolding of the mediant recursion exhausts the closed form at rate `(2/3)ᵈ`, uniformly over all nodes — the error at depth `d` is exactly the frontier mass, and each generation retains at most `2/3` of its parent mass.

declarations.name: tendsto_sternBrocotDepthMass
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_sternBrocotDepthMass (a b : ℕ+) : Filter.Tendsto (fun dp : ℕ => sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b) Filter.atTop (nhds (cylinderMass a b))
declarations.docstring: **The Stern–Brocot cylinder law, limit form**: the depth-`d` unfolding of the mediant recursion at `(a,b)` converges to the closed form `M(a,b) = 1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1))`, with explicit rate `(2/3)ᵈ` from `sternBrocotDepthMass_error`. At the root: `M(1,1) = 1`, matching the reduced-direction law `tsum_pos_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one`.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffSeries
declarations.signature: noncomputable def binaryCoeffSeries (c : ℕ → ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The binary coefficient series `X_c = ∑_{n≥1} c(n)/2^n`.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail
declarations.signature: noncomputable def binaryCoeffTail (c : ℕ → ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The scaled tail `T_c(N) = ∑_{j≥1} c(N+j)/2^j`.

declarations.name: HasRationalValue
declarations.docstring: A real number represented by an integer numerator and a positive natural denominator. This is the explicit positive-denominator form of membership in `ℚ`; it keeps the carry multiplier visible in theorem statements.

declarations.name: hasRationalValue_iff_not_irrational
declarations.signature: theorem hasRationalValue_iff_not_irrational (x : ℝ) : HasRationalValue x ↔ ¬ Irrational x
declarations.docstring: The explicit positive-denominator predicate is exactly standard rationality (`¬ Irrational x`).

declarations.name: IsTemperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: def IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (v : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The exact integer recurrence together with the subexponential boundary `u(N) = o(2^N)`, expressed as convergence of the quotient.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem binaryCoeffTail_zero (c : ℕ → ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail c 0 = binaryCoeffSeries c
declarations.docstring: The scaled tail at `N = 0` is the original coefficient series.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_nonneg (c : ℕ → ℕ) (N : ℕ) : 0 ≤ binaryCoeffTail c N
declarations.docstring: Nonnegative coefficients give a nonnegative scaled tail.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_le (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail c N ≤ (N : ℝ) + 2
declarations.docstring: A linearly bounded coefficient sequence has the tail bound `T_c(N) ≤ N+2`.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_div_pow_tendsto_zero
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_div_pow_tendsto_zero (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ binaryCoeffTail c N / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: The scaled coefficient tail itself is subexponential.

declarations.name: scaledTail_div_pow_tendsto_zero
declarations.signature: private theorem scaledTail_div_pow_tendsto_zero (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (v : ℕ) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail c N / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: The scaled-tail quotient `v*T_c(N) / 2^N` inherits the subexponential decay. Shared tendsto-transfer step for the rigidity and existence proofs.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_succ
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_succ (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail c (N + 1) = 2 * binaryCoeffTail c N - (c (N + 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Doubling the tail shifts the window by one and expels its head coefficient.

declarations.name: doublingOrbit_eq_zero_of_tempered
declarations.signature: theorem doublingOrbit_eq_zero_of_tempered (d : ℕ → ℝ) (hrec : ∀ N : ℕ, d (N + 1) = 2 * d N) (htemp : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ d N / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)) : ∀ N : ℕ, d N = 0
declarations.docstring: A real orbit satisfying `d(N+1)=2d(N)` and `d(N)=o(2^N)` is identically zero. This is the abstract rigidity engine.

declarations.name: temperedBinaryOrbit_eq_scaledTail
declarations.signature: theorem temperedBinaryOrbit_eq_scaledTail (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u) : ∀ N : ℕ, (u N : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail c N
declarations.docstring: **Tail-orbit rigidity.** Every tempered integer orbit is the canonical scaled tail `v*T_c`.

declarations.name: exists_temperedBinaryOrbit_of_rational
declarations.signature: theorem exists_temperedBinaryOrbit_of_rational (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (hrat : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c)) : ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: Rationality supplies the canonical positive-multiplier tempered integer orbit.

declarations.name: rational_of_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem rational_of_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (horbit : ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u) : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c)
declarations.docstring: A tempered integer orbit forces rationality of the coefficient series.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c) ↔ ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: **Subexponential tail-orbit rigidity, equivalence form.** The series `X_c` is rational exactly when some tempered integer orbit exists.

declarations.name: not_irrational_binaryCoeffSeries_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem not_irrational_binaryCoeffSeries_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : ¬ Irrational (binaryCoeffSeries c) ↔ ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: The equivalence in Mathlib's standard irrationality vocabulary (`¬ Irrational`).

declarations.name: card_antidiagonal_filter_pos_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem card_antidiagonal_filter_pos_coprime (n : ℕ) : ((Finset.antidiagonal n).filter fun p : ℕ × ℕ => 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2).card = Nat.totient n
declarations.docstring: **Visible-point count = totient, uniformly in `n`.** The half-open filter (`0 < a`, `b` free) makes the identity hypothesis-free: `n = 0` gives the empty filter (`φ(0) = 0`), `n = 1` gives exactly `(1,0)` (`φ(1) = 1`), and for `n ≥ 2` the pair `(a, n-a)` is coprime iff `a` is a totient witness of `n`.

declarations.name: summable_pow_add
declarations.signature: theorem summable_pow_add {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => r ^ (p.1 + p.2))
declarations.docstring: **Visible-point count = totient, uniformly in `n`.** The half-open filter (`0 < a`, `b` free) makes the identity hypothesis-free: `n = 0` gives the empty filter (`φ(0) = 0`), `n = 1` gives exactly `(1,0)` (`φ(1) = 1`), and for `n ≥ 2` the pair `(a, n-a)` is coprime iff `a` is a totient witness of `n`. -/ theorem card_antidiagonal_filter_pos_coprime (n : ℕ) : ((Finset.antidiagonal n).filter fun p : ℕ × ℕ => 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2).card = Nat.totient n := by rw [Nat.totient_eq_card_coprime] refine Finset.card_bij' (fun p _ => p.2) (fun a _ => (n - a, a)) ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ · intro p hp dsimp only rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_antidiagonal] at hp obtain ⟨hsum, hpos, hcop⟩ := hp rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_range] refine ⟨by omega, ?_⟩ rw [← hsum] exact Nat.coprime_add_self_left.mpr hcop · intro a ha dsimp only rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_range] at ha obtain ⟨hlt, hcop⟩ := ha rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_antidiagonal] refine ⟨by omega, by omega, ?_⟩ exact (Nat.coprime_s

declarations.name: summable_coprime_pair_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0)

declarations.name: summable_pos_coprime_pair_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_pos_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0)

declarations.name: tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n
declarations.docstring: **Half-open visible-coprime bridge.** Regrouping the coprime-pair power sum along addition antidiagonals turns the visible-point count into the totient coefficient: the #249 power series IS a lattice-point mass.

declarations.name: halfopen_split
declarations.signature: private lemma halfopen_split (r : ℝ) (p : ℕ × ℕ) : (if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) + (if p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) then r else 0)
declarations.docstring: **Half-open visible-coprime bridge.** Regrouping the coprime-pair power sum along addition antidiagonals turns the visible-point count into the totient coefficient: the #249 power series IS a lattice-point mass. -/ theorem tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n := by have hFsum := summable_coprime_pair_pow hr0 hr1 have hsig : Summable (fun x : (Σ n : ℕ, {y // y ∈ Finset.antidiagonal n}) => if 0 < (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 ∧ Nat.Coprime (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).2 then r ^ ((x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 + (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).2) else 0) := by have h := (Equiv.summable_iff Finset.sigmaAntidiagonalEquivProd).mpr hFsum exact h.congr fun x => rfl calc (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' x : (Σ n : ℕ, {y // y ∈ Finset.antidiagonal n}), (if 0 < (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 ∧ Nat.Coprime (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).2 then r ^

declarations.name: tsum_pos_coprime_pair_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n) - r
declarations.docstring: **Positive visible-coprime bridge**: over strictly positive coprime pairs, `∑ r^(a+b) = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ - r`. At `r = 1/2` the left side is the probability that two independent fair-coin waiting times are coprime, and the boundary term `r` that separates it from the #249 constant is `P(X = 1) = L(μ)` — the ladder's Möbius rung.

declarations.name: summable_totient_mul_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_totient_mul_pow {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : Summable (fun n : ℕ => (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * x ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Positive visible-coprime bridge**: over strictly positive coprime pairs, `∑ r^(a+b) = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ - r`. At `r = 1/2` the left side is the probability that two independent fair-coin waiting times are coprime, and the boundary term `r` that separates it from the #249 constant is `P(X = 1) = L(μ)` — the ladder's Möbius rung. -/ theorem tsum_pos_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n) - r := by have hpos := summable_pos_coprime_pair_pow hr0 hr1 have hedge : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => if p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) then r else 0) := by refine Summable.of_nonneg_of_le (fun p => ?_) (fun p => ?_) (summable_pow_add hr0 hr1) · by_cases h : p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) · rw [if_pos h]; exact hr0 · rw [if_neg h] · by_cases h : p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) · subst h simp · rw [if_neg h] positivity have hhalf := tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow hr0 hr1 have hsp

declarations.name: tsum_totient_mul_pow_sub_self
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_totient_mul_pow_sub_self {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * x ^ n) - x = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient (n + 2) : ℝ) * x ^ (n + 2)

declarations.name: tsum_pow_succ_geometric
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pow_succ_geometric {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : ∑' g : ℕ, x ^ (g + 1) = x / (1 - x)

declarations.name: tsum_pnat_mul_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pnat_mul_geometric {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' n : ℕ+, ((n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ) = r / (1 - r) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: `∑_{n≥1} n·rⁿ = r/(1-r)²` — the generic-`r` value of the totient Lambert series (wave-18 `tsum_totient_lambert` lands here through `φ * ζ = Id`).

declarations.name: summable_totient_lambert_shift
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_totient_lambert_shift {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (Nat.totient (j + 1) : ℝ) * (r ^ (j + 1) / (1 - r ^ (j + 1))))

declarations.name: tsum_totient_lambert_tail
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_lambert_tail {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient (n + 2) : ℝ) * (r ^ (n + 2) / (1 - r ^ (n + 2))) = r / (1 - r) ^ 2 - r / (1 - r)
declarations.docstring: **Tail Lambert evaluation**: `∑_{n≥2} φ(n)·rⁿ/(1-rⁿ) = r/(1-r)² - r/(1-r)`. This is the wave-18 rung `L(φ)` with its `d = 1` term peeled off — the analytic identity behind the gcd-layer normalization.
