declarations.name: tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' g : ℕ, (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then (r ^ (g + 1)) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (r / (1 - r)) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **The gcd-layer decomposition, generic `0 ≤ r < 1`.** Every positive pair `(a,b)` factors uniquely as `g·(a',b')` with `gcd(a',b') = 1`, so summing the positive coprime-pair mass at every scale `r, r², r³, …` recovers the full positive-quadrant mass `(∑_{a≥1} rᵃ)² = (r/(1-r))²`. Layer `g` is the mass of `{gcd = g}`; the `g = 1` layer at `r = 1/2` is the open #249 value `S - 1/2`.

declarations.name: tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_half_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_half_eq_one : ∑' g : ℕ, (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (g + 1)) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The fair-coin gcd law is exactly normalized.** At `r = 1/2` the layer masses are genuine probabilities — layer `g` is `P(gcd(X,Y) = g+1)` for independent fair-coin waiting times — and they sum to `1`: the gcd is finite almost surely. Through the visible-coprime bridge this is precisely the probabilistic content of the wave-18 rational rung `L(φ) = 2`.

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRat
declarations.docstring: The exact rational Mersenne weight `1 / (2^n - 1)`. Its meaningful support indices are positive; at index zero Lean's division convention gives zero.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight
declarations.docstring: The real Mersenne weight `1 / (2^n - 1)`.

declarations.name: cast_mersenneWeightRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem cast_mersenneWeightRat (n : ℕ) : ((mersenneWeightRat n : ℚ) : ℝ) = mersenneWeight n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRat_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRat_pos {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 < mersenneWeightRat n

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeight_pos {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 < mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: Real positivity is the cast image of the exact rational proof.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_nonneg
declarations.signature: private theorem mersenneWeight_nonneg (n : ℕ) : 0 ≤ mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: The real weight is nonnegative at every index, including the normalized-away index zero.

declarations.name: summable_mersenneWeight
declarations.signature: theorem summable_mersenneWeight : Summable (fun k : ℕ => mersenneWeight (k + 1))
declarations.docstring: The positive-index Mersenne weights form a summable real series.

declarations.name: summable_shift_add
declarations.signature: private theorem summable_shift_add {f : ℕ → ℝ} (hf : Summable fun k : ℕ => f (k + 1)) (n : ℕ) : Summable fun k : ℕ => f (n + k + 1)
declarations.docstring: Shifting the start of a summable positive-index family preserves summability; shared by the tail, suffix, and envelope constructions.

declarations.name: summable_geometric_shift_add
declarations.signature: private theorem summable_geometric_shift_add {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) (n : ℕ) : Summable fun k : ℕ => r ^ (n + k + 1)
declarations.docstring: The shifted geometric envelope shared by all three channel estimates.

declarations.name: mersenneTail
declarations.docstring: The remaining mass after processing exponents `1, ..., n`.

declarations.name: summable_mersenneTail
declarations.signature: theorem summable_mersenneTail (n : ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => mersenneWeight (n + k + 1))

declarations.name: mersenneTail_eq_weight_add
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_eq_weight_add (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n = mersenneWeight (n + 1) + mersenneTail (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Splitting off the next positive exponent gives the exact tail recurrence.

declarations.name: two_mul_mersenneWeight_succ_lt
declarations.signature: theorem two_mul_mersenneWeight_succ_lt {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 2 * mersenneWeight (n + 1) < mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: Adjacent weights shrink by strictly more than a factor of two.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_add_le_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeight_add_le_geometric (n j : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneWeight (n + j) ≤ mersenneWeight n * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ j
declarations.docstring: Iterating the adjacent ratio gives a geometric envelope.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_le_two_mul_weight
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_le_two_mul_weight (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n ≤ 2 * mersenneWeight (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: A simple closed-form upper enclosure for the remaining infinite tail.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_lt_weight
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_lt_weight {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneTail n < mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: **Strict superincreasingness.** Every positive-index weight is larger than the entire tail following it.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_lt_two_mul_weight
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_lt_two_mul_weight (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n < 2 * mersenneWeight (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: The one-step enclosure is in fact strict.

declarations.name: tendsto_mersenneTail_zero
declarations.docstring: The remaining Mersenne mass tends to zero.

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder
declarations.docstring: The contribution of geometric channels of order at least three in one Mersenne weight.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_eq_two_channels_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeight_eq_two_channels_add_remainder {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneWeight n = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n + ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n + mersenneWeightRemainder n
declarations.docstring: Exact separation of the first two geometric channels.

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainder_nonneg {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 ≤ mersenneWeightRemainder n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder_le
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainder_le {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneWeightRemainder n ≤ 2 * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n
declarations.docstring: Uniform third-channel bound for one weight.

declarations.name: tsum_geometric_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_geometric_nat_add_succ (r : ℝ) (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, r ^ (n + k + 1) = r ^ (n + 1) * (1 - r)⁻¹
declarations.docstring: A shifted geometric series in the form used by all three channels.

declarations.name: tsum_half_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_half_nat_add_succ (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n + k + 1) = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n

declarations.name: tsum_quarter_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_quarter_nat_add_succ (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ (n + k + 1) = (1 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n

declarations.name: tsum_eighth_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_eighth_nat_add_succ (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ (n + k + 1) = (1 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainderTail
declarations.docstring: The accumulated contribution of channels of order at least three after exponent `n`.

declarations.name: summable_mersenneWeightRemainderTail
declarations.signature: theorem summable_mersenneWeightRemainderTail (n : ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => mersenneWeightRemainder (n + k + 1))

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainderTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainderTail_le (n : ℕ) : mersenneWeightRemainderTail n ≤ (2 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n
declarations.docstring: The accumulated higher-channel tail is at most `(2/7)·8⁻ⁿ`.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_eq_two_channels_add_remainderTail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_eq_two_channels_add_remainderTail (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n + (1 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n + mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: Exact tail expansion through the second geometric channel.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_sub_main_eq_remainder_sub_tail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_sub_main_eq_remainder_sub_tail {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : (mersenneWeight n - mersenneTail n) - (2 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n = mersenneWeightRemainder n - mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne gap has leading term `(2/3)·4⁻ⁿ`; its exact error is the difference of the one-weight and tail higher-channel remainders.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_asymptotic_bound
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_asymptotic_bound {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : ‖(mersenneWeight n - mersenneTail n) - (2 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n‖ ≤ 3 * ‖((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n‖
declarations.docstring: Explicit norm certificate for the gap asymptotic.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_isBigOWith
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_isBigOWith : Asymptotics.IsBigOWith 3 atTop (fun n : ℕ => (mersenneWeight n - mersenneTail n) - (2 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n) (fun n : ℕ => ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Gap asymptotic.** In positive indexing, `w_n - T_n = (2/3)·4⁻ⁿ + O(8⁻ⁿ)`, with an explicit valid `O` constant `3`.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveMersenneSupportValue (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The value coded by a set of positive exponents. The sequence index is zero-based while the exponent supplied to the weight is `k+1`.

declarations.name: summable_positiveMersenneSupportIndicator
declarations.signature: theorem summable_positiveMersenneSupportIndicator (A : Set ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (k + 1))

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The portion of a support code strictly after exponent `n`.

declarations.name: summable_positiveMersenneSupportSuffix
declarations.signature: theorem summable_positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (n + k + 1))

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_nonneg (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : 0 ≤ positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_le_tail
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_le_tail (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: A coded suffix is bounded by the full remaining mass.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_eq_indicator_add
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_eq_indicator_add (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n = Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (n + 1) + positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact recurrence for a coded support suffix.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_prefix_add_suffix
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_prefix_add_suffix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (k + 1)) + positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n
declarations.docstring: Finite-prefix decomposition of a normalized support code.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_erdosSupportSeries
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_erdosSupportSeries (A : Set ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = erdosSupportSeries 2 A
declarations.docstring: The reindexed support value is exactly the certificate kernel's #257 support series.

declarations.name: mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne achievement set, with the analytically invisible zero bit normalized away.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneRemainder (x : ℝ) : ℕ → ℝ | 0 => x | n + 1 => if mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n then greedyMersenneRemainder x n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainder x n /-- Exact rational version of the greedy residual. -/ def greedyMersenneRemainderRat (x : ℚ) : ℕ → ℚ | 0 => x | n + 1 => if mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n then greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Real greedy residual after processing exponents `1, ..., n`.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat
declarations.signature: def greedyMersenneRemainderRat (x : ℚ) : ℕ → ℚ | 0 => x | n + 1 => if mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n then greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n @[simp] theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_zero (x : ℝ) : greedyMersenneRemainder x 0 = x
declarations.docstring: Exact rational version of the greedy residual.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_zero (x : ℝ) : greedyMersenneRemainder x 0 = x

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem greedyMersenneRemainderRat_zero (x : ℚ) : greedyMersenneRemainderRat x 0 = x

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_succ (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainder x (n + 1) = if mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n then greedyMersenneRemainder x n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainder x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainderRat_succ (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainderRat x (n + 1) = if mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n then greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_nonneg {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (n : ℕ) : 0 ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n
declarations.docstring: Greedy subtraction preserves nonnegativity.

declarations.name: cast_greedyMersenneRemainderRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem cast_greedyMersenneRemainderRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : ((greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n : ℚ) : ℝ) = greedyMersenneRemainder (x : ℝ) n
declarations.docstring: Rational and real greedy execution agree exactly under casting.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSupport
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneSupport (x : ℝ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The set of positive exponents selected by the real greedy recursion.

declarations.name: succ_mem_greedyMersenneSupport_iff
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem succ_mem_greedyMersenneSupport_iff (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ greedyMersenneSupport x ↔ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenne_prefix_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenne_prefix_add_remainder (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, Set.indicator (greedyMersenneSupport x) mersenneWeight (k + 1)) + greedyMersenneRemainder x n
declarations.docstring: Exact finite-prefix identity for the real greedy recursion.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_supportValue
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_supportValue (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainder (positiveMersenneSupportValue A) n = positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n
declarations.docstring: On a genuine support value, the greedy residual is exactly the unprocessed support suffix.

declarations.name: succ_mem_greedySupport_supportValue_iff
declarations.signature: theorem succ_mem_greedySupport_supportValue_iff (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ greedyMersenneSupport (positiveMersenneSupportValue A) ↔ n + 1 ∈ A
declarations.docstring: Greedy selection recovers every positive bit of a representing support.

declarations.name: greedySupport_supportValue_eq
declarations.signature: theorem greedySupport_supportValue_eq (A : Set ℕ) (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) : greedyMersenneSupport (positiveMersenneSupportValue A) = A
declarations.docstring: A normalized support is recovered exactly, including the forced-zero bit at exponent zero.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_nonneg (A : Set ℕ) : 0 ≤ positiveMersenneSupportValue A
declarations.docstring: Every support value is nonnegative.

declarations.name: greedy_survives_of_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.signature: theorem greedy_survives_of_mem_mersenneAchievementSet {x : ℝ} (hx : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet) : 0 ≤ x ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: Membership implies the exact all-level upper-tail survival inequalities.

declarations.name: tendsto_greedyMersenneRemainder_zero
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_greedyMersenneRemainder_zero {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (hsurvive : ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n) : Tendsto (greedyMersenneRemainder x) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: If every greedy residual stays at or below the remaining mass, the residuals converge to zero. The nonnegativity guard excludes the false negative-target variant.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_greedy_survival
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_greedy_survival {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (hsurvive : ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: All-level survival constructs a genuine normalized support code.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival (x : ℝ) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet ↔ 0 ≤ x ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: **Greedy membership criterion.** A real target belongs to the Mersenne achievement set exactly when it is nonnegative and every residual is at most the full remaining tail.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_injective_normalized
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_injective_normalized {A B : Set ℕ} (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (hB0 : 0 ∉ B) (hvalue : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = positiveMersenneSupportValue B) : A = B
declarations.docstring: Strict superincreasingness makes normalized support coding injective.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_two_injective_normalized
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_two_injective_normalized {A B : Set ℕ} (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (hB0 : 0 ∉ B) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = erdosSupportSeries 2 B) : A = B
declarations.docstring: The support coding through the existing #257 series is likewise unique after the zero-bit normalization.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_eq_prefix_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_eq_prefix_add_tail (n lookahead : ℕ) : mersenneTail n = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range lookahead, mersenneWeight (n + k + 1)) + mersenneTail (n + lookahead)
declarations.docstring: Split a tail after any finite lookahead.

declarations.name: mersenneTailUpperRat
declarations.docstring: A finite rational upper enclosure for `mersenneTail n`. The lookahead parameter exposes a family of exact rational bounds without comparing an infinite real tail numerically.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_lt_cast_tailUpperRat
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_lt_cast_tailUpperRat (n lookahead : ℕ) : mersenneTail n < ((mersenneTailUpperRat n lookahead : ℚ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Every finite rational enclosure lies strictly above the actual tail.

declarations.name: GreedyMersenneDeath
declarations.docstring: Semantic greedy death: the residual at the given level is already larger than all remaining available mass.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneDeath_not_mem
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneDeath_not_mem {x : ℝ} {level : ℕ} (hdeath : GreedyMersenneDeath x level) : x ∉ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Any semantic death level proves nonmembership.

declarations.name: CertifiedGreedyMersenneDeath
declarations.signature: def CertifiedGreedyMersenneDeath (x : ℚ) (level lookahead : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A decidable finite certificate: an exact rational upper enclosure is no larger than the exact rational greedy residual.

declarations.name: certifiedGreedyMersenneDeath_not_mem
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedGreedyMersenneDeath_not_mem {x : ℚ} {level lookahead : ℕ} (hcert : CertifiedGreedyMersenneDeath x level lookahead) : ((x : ℚ) : ℝ) ∉ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Every finite exact-rational death certificate is sound.

declarations.name: three_fourths_certifiedGreedyMersenneDeath
declarations.docstring: **Exact level-one certificate.** Greedy skips weight `w₁=1` at `3/4`, leaving residual `3/4`; the zero-lookahead enclosure of the remaining tail is `2*w₂ = 2/3`.

declarations.name: three_fourths_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Consequently `3/4` is not in the Mersenne achievement set.

declarations.name: greedyMersennePrefixRat
declarations.signature: def greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : Finset ℕ
declarations.docstring: Positive exponents selected through a finite exact-rational greedy run.

declarations.name: zero_not_mem_greedyMersennePrefixRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem zero_not_mem_greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : 0 ∉ greedyMersennePrefixRat x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRat_prefix_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRat_prefix_add_remainder (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, if mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x k then mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) else 0) + greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n
declarations.docstring: Exact telescoping identity for a finite rational greedy run.

declarations.name: of
declarations.signature: instance of the certificate kernel's `finiteErdosSum`. -/ theorem finiteErdosSum_greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : finiteErdosSum (greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) 2 = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, if mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x k then mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) else 0

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum_greedyMersennePrefixRat
declarations.signature: theorem finiteErdosSum_greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : finiteErdosSum (greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) 2 = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, if mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x k then mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) else 0
declarations.docstring: The finite prefix selected by the rational greedy run is exactly an instance of the certificate kernel's `finiteErdosSum`.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat_eq_sub_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainderRat_eq_sub_finiteErdosSum (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n = x - finiteErdosSum (greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) 2
declarations.docstring: Exact interface to the certificate kernel: the rational greedy residual is the target minus a `finiteErdosSum`.

declarations.name: rational_greedy_take_iff_real
declarations.signature: theorem rational_greedy_take_iff_real (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n ↔ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (x : ℝ) n
declarations.docstring: A rational comparison decides exactly the same greedy bit as the real comparison after casting.

declarations.name: rational_member_support_bit_iff
declarations.signature: theorem rational_member_support_bit_iff (x : ℚ) (A : Set ℕ) (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (hvalue : ((x : ℚ) : ℝ) = positiveMersenneSupportValue A) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ A ↔ mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n
declarations.docstring: A rational value already known to have normalized support has computable support bits, recovered by the exact rational greedy recurrence. This does not assert decidability of achievement-set membership.

declarations.name: half_sub_five_term_prefix_eq
declarations.signature: theorem half_sub_five_term_prefix_eq : (1 / 2 : ℚ) - finiteErdosSum ({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) 2 = 1 / 688086
declarations.docstring: An exact worked prefix identity. Selecting exponents `{2,3,6,7,14}` from the target `1/2` leaves the rational residual `1/688086`.

declarations.name: periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm
declarations.signature: lemma periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm {t u : ℕ} (htu : t ≤ u) : periodLcm t ∣ periodLcm u
declarations.docstring: `lcm(1..t) ∣ lcm(1..u)` for `t ≤ u`: the cones at all scales fit into one directed system, so every cross-scale pair `(q·H_t, r·H_u)` is a same-scale pair on the `t`-ray.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) : ∃ t₁ : ℕ, ∀ t, t₁ ≤ t → ∀ q m : ℕ, 0 < q → totientTail (q * periodLcm t + m * periodLcm t) - totientTail (q * periodLcm t) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-cone flatness law.** If the totient series is rational, then from some scale on, the ENTIRE lcm cone is fractionally flat: every difference `R_{q·H_t + m·H_t} - R_{q·H_t}` is an integer, for every `t` past the threshold, every base multiplier `q ≥ 1`, and every step multiplier `m`. One constant fractional part governs every cone point — the maximal spend of the rationality hypothesis on the lcm lattice.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ certifiedKill (m * periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-24 cone collapse.** #249 follows from certified kills anywhere on the two-multiplier lcm cone: `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t`. Subsumes the wave-23 diagonal (`q = m = 1`), every q-ray step (`m = 1`), every q-gap (free `m`), and — via `periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm` — every cross-scale pair, since `H_u` is itself a multiple of `H_t` for `u ≥ t`. Rationality flattens the cone; one certified annihilator at arbitrarily large scale is a contradiction.

declarations.name: summable_linear_half
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_linear_half' : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (j : ℝ) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-24 cone collapse.** #249 follows from certified kills anywhere on the two-multiplier lcm cone: `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t`. Subsumes the wave-23 diagonal (`q = m = 1`), every q-ray step (`m = 1`), every q-gap (free `m`), and — via `periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm` — every cross-scale pair, since `H_u` is itself a multiple of `H_t` for `u ≥ t`. Rationality flattens the cone; one certified annihilator at arbitrarily large scale is a contradiction. -/ theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ certifiedKill (m * periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) := by by_contra hrat obtain ⟨t₁, hflat⟩ := rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness hrat obtain ⟨t, ht, q, m, L, hq, hkill⟩ := hsupply t₁ exact tail_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedKill hkill (hflat t ht q m hq) /-! ## Certificate completeness: kills exist exactly at non-integers

declarations.name: summable_const_add_mul_half
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_const_add_mul_half' (C : ℝ) : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (C + (j : ℝ)) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j)

declarations.name: exists_depth_lt
declarations.signature: private lemma exists_depth_lt (C δ : ℝ) (hδ : 0 < δ) : ∃ L : ℕ, (C + (L : ℝ)) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ L < δ

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_tail_diff_notMem_int {h N : ℕ} (hD : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness (the converse of wave 21).** If the tail difference `R_{N+h} - R_N` is a non-integer, some finite depth certifies it: its distance `δ` to the nearest integer is positive, and any window depth `L` with `2·(N+h+L+2) < δ·2^L` places the residue outside the forbidden band.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int (h N : ℕ) : (∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) ↔ totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness, iff form.** Some depth certifies `(h, N)` exactly when the tail difference is a non-integer. Finite certificates are receipts; non-integrality is the object.

declarations.name: periodLcm_diagonal_kill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem periodLcm_diagonal_kill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int (t : ℕ) : (∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) ↔ totientTail (periodLcm t + periodLcm t) - totientTail (periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The wave-23 predicate `P t` in pure form: a diagonal certificate depth exists iff the diagonal tail difference is a non-integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ totientTail (periodLcm t + periodLcm t) - totientTail (periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The diagonal frontier without certificate vocabulary.** If the diagonal tail difference `R_{2H_t} - R_{H_t}` is a non-integer for arbitrarily large `t`, the totient series is irrational. (Completeness converts each non-integrality into a finite decidable certificate.)

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ totientTail (q * periodLcm t + m * periodLcm t) - totientTail (q * periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone frontier in pure form**: one non-integral cone difference at arbitrarily large scale proves #249 — no certificate vocabulary at all.

declarations.name: windowDiscrepancy2
declarations.signature: def windowDiscrepancy2 (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Second-difference window discrepancy `A₂ = A(h, N+h, L) - A(h, N, L)`: the depth-`L` truncation of `2^L·((R_{N+2h} - R_{N+h}) - (R_{N+h} - R_N))`.

declarations.name: certifiedRank2Kill
declarations.signature: def certifiedRank2Kill (h N L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The decidable rank-2 certificate: the residue of `A₂` modulo `2^L` avoids the radius-`2(N+2h+L+2)` neighbourhood of `0`. The doubled radius pays for two window truncations; in exchange the second difference cancels the whole `H·C` clean shadow on the lcm cone.

declarations.name: second_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedRank2Kill
declarations.signature: theorem second_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedRank2Kill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedRank2Kill h N L) : (totientTail (N + 2 * h) - totientTail (N + h)) - (totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 soundness.** A certified rank-2 residue proves the second difference `(R_{N+2h} - R_{N+h}) - (R_{N+h} - R_N)` is not an integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank2_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank2_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ certifiedRank2Kill (periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 cone supply.** Certified second-difference kills along the q-rays of the lcm cone — `(h, N) = (H_t, q·H_t)` — at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249: rationality flattens the cone, so BOTH first differences are forced integral and their difference is an integer.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_lcm_cone_cells
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_lcm_cone_cells : certifiedKill (1 * periodLcm 2) (3 * periodLcm 2) 6 ∧ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 3) (2 * periodLcm 3) 7 ∧ certifiedKill (3 * periodLcm 3) (1 * periodLcm 3) 8
declarations.docstring: Cone-lattice deposits beyond the diagonal: kills at `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` for `(t, q, m) = (2, 3, 1)`, `(3, 2, 2)`, `(3, 1, 3)` — the first machine-checked annihilators off the diagonal, witnessing the cone supply shape at its probe-tabulated depths.

declarations.name: certifiedRank2Kill_example
declarations.docstring: A machine-checked rank-2 kill: the second difference at `(h, N) = (1, 8)` is certified non-integral at depth `9` (totient arguments ≤ 19).

declarations.name: rank2_kill_sound_but_not_shallower_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem rank2_kill_sound_but_not_shallower_at_cell : certifiedKill 1 8 8 ∧ (∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ certifiedRank2Kill 1 8 L) ∧ certifiedRank2Kill 1 8 9
declarations.docstring: **The measured wave-24 verdict, kernel-checked at the fixture cell**: rank-2 is SOUND but NOT shallower. At `(h, N) = (1, 8)` the rank-1 certificate fires at depth `8` while no rank-2 certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8`. (Probe, `t ≤ 20`: rank-1 at least as shallow in 30 of 40 cells, median depth delta `+1` against rank-2 — the advisory-1 promotion gate is measured and denied.)

declarations.name: second_diff_not_int_example
declarations.signature: theorem second_diff_not_int_example : (totientTail (8 + 2 * 1) - totientTail (8 + 1)) - (totientTail (8 + 1) - totientTail 8) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Concrete rank-2 non-integrality: `(R₁₀ - R₉) - (R₉ - R₈)` is not an integer — the first machine-checked second-difference obstruction.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal_seven_eight
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal_seven_eight : (totientTail (periodLcm 7 + periodLcm 7) - totientTail (periodLcm 7) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) ∧ (totientTail (periodLcm 8 + periodLcm 8) - totientTail (periodLcm 8) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The diagonal tail differences at `t = 7` and `t = 8` are non-integers — extending the wave-23 corollary past its `t ≤ 6` decide ceiling via the `_at_seven` / `_at_eight` deposits.

declarations.name: plus
declarations.signature: structure plus a non-negative fresh-prime (`> t`) defect. It is NOT a supply step: it does not fire any certificate, it fixes the arithmetic of the window argument. -/ theorem small_prime_dvd_cone_window_iff_dvd_offset {t q s p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpt : p ≤ t) : p ∣ q * periodLcm t + s ↔ p ∣ s

declarations.name: small_prime_dvd_cone_window_iff_dvd_offset
declarations.signature: theorem small_prime_dvd_cone_window_iff_dvd_offset {t q s p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpt : p ≤ t) : p ∣ q * periodLcm t + s ↔ p ∣ s

declarations.name: windowNumerator
declarations.docstring: The depth-`L` window numerator `P_L(M) = Σ_{j<L} φ(M+1+j)·2^{L-1-j}`: the integer layer of `2^L·R_M`, exact up to the one-sided deep tail `0 ≤ 2^L·R_M - P_L(M) ≤ M+L+2`.

declarations.name: windowNumerator_div_eq
declarations.signature: lemma windowNumerator_div_eq (M L : ℕ) : ((windowNumerator M L : ℕ) : ℝ) / 2 ^ L = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)
declarations.docstring: The window numerator over `2^L` is the depth-`L` partial tail.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert
declarations.signature: def coneNonflatCert (H L : ℕ) (Q : List ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: **The cone non-flatness certificate.** For the vertex menu `Q` at scale `H` and depth `L`: every left endpoint `P_L(qᵢ·H)` escapes some arc `[P_L(qⱼ·H), P_L(qⱼ·H) + (qⱼ·H+L+2)]` modulo `2^L`. A `|Q|²` one-sided window check; decidable.

declarations.name: exists_nonintegral_pair_of_coneNonflatCert
declarations.signature: theorem exists_nonintegral_pair_of_coneNonflatCert {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : ∃ qi ∈ Q, ∃ qj ∈ Q, totientTail (qj * H) - totientTail (qi * H) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Menu refutation soundness.** If the certificate fires and the one-sided floor `2^L > q·H+L+2` holds at every vertex, then some pair of menu vertices has a NON-integral tail difference. Proof shape: were every pair integral, the vertex `q⋆` of minimal deep tail would give, for every menu vertex `q`, the integer `C = P(q⋆·H) - P(q·H) + k·2^L` equal to the real number `(T_q - T_{q⋆})·2^L ∈ [0, q·H+L+2]` — so the left endpoint `P(q⋆·H)` sits inside EVERY arc, and the certificate row at `q⋆` is exactly the denial of that.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, ∃ Q : List ℕ, Q ≠ [] ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, 0 < q) ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, (q * periodLcm t + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) ∧ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm t) L Q) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone non-flatness supply theorem.** If for arbitrarily large `t` some finite positive vertex menu on the `lcm(1..t)` cone fires the non-flatness certificate (with its one-sided floors), the totient series is irrational. Rationality flattens the cone — every pair of menu vertices would have integral difference — and the refuter denies all of them at once.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, certifiedKill (periodLcm (t + 1) - periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Jump supply.** Certified kills at the lcm jumps — `(h, N) = (H_{t+1} - H_t, H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t` — prove #249. Each jump is the cone cell `(q, m) = (1, H_{t+1}/H_t - 1)`; no side condition is needed because `H_t ∣ H_{t+1}` makes the multiplier exact (and a `h = 0` window can never be certified).

declarations.name: coneCommonPoint
declarations.signature: def coneCommonPoint (H L : ℕ) (Q : List ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A **common point** of the depth-`L` menu arcs: an integer residue whose one-sided distance from every left endpoint `P_L(q·H)` stays inside that vertex's arc radius `q·H+L+2` modulo `2^L`. Joint flatness of the menu would pin `2^L·μ` at exactly such a point.

declarations.name: windowNumerator_succ
declarations.signature: lemma windowNumerator_succ (M L : ℕ) : windowNumerator M (L + 1) = 2 * windowNumerator M L + Nat.totient (M + 1 + L)
declarations.docstring: The window recursion: one more depth bit appends one totient value, `P_{L+1}(M) = 2·P_L(M) + φ(M+L+1)`.

declarations.name: not_coneCommonPoint_of_coneNonflatCert
declarations.signature: theorem not_coneCommonPoint_of_coneNonflatCert {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : ¬ coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Refuter soundness against common points**: a firing certificate denies every common point — the combinatorial argmin. If `y` were common, the vertex `q⋆` of minimal one-sided distance to `y` would have its left endpoint inside EVERY arc, and `q⋆`'s certificate row denies exactly that.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_of_not_coneCommonPoint
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_of_not_coneCommonPoint {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (h : ¬ coneCommonPoint H L Q) : coneNonflatCert H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Refuter completeness against common points**: if no common point exists, the certificate fires — a silent row at `qi` would make the left endpoint `P_L(qi·H)` itself a common point.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_iff_no_common_point
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_iff_no_common_point {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) : coneNonflatCert H L Q ↔ ¬ coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Exactness of the menu refuter, kernel-checked**: the certificate fires iff the menu arcs share NO common point. This is the law the probe gate `nonflat_matches_common_point_bruteforce` measured at every tested cell; it is now a theorem at every cell.

declarations.name: coneCommonPoint_of_succ
declarations.signature: theorem coneCommonPoint_of_succ {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (h : coneCommonPoint H (L + 1) Q) : coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Halving descent**: a common point at depth `L+1` yields one at depth `L` (floors held at `L`). The reduced witness halves; the window recursion turns the arc bookkeeping into `⌊(φ + b)/2⌋ ≤ q·H+L+2`.

declarations.name: coneNonflat_floor_succ
declarations.signature: lemma coneNonflat_floor_succ {H q L : ℕ} (h : (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) : (q * H + (L + 1) + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ (L + 1)
declarations.docstring: The one-sided floor propagates up one depth bit.

declarations.name: coneNonflat_floor_mono
declarations.signature: lemma coneNonflat_floor_mono {H q L L' : ℕ} (hle : L ≤ L') (h : (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) : (q * H + L' + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L'
declarations.docstring: The one-sided floor propagates to every larger depth.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_mono_depth
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_mono_depth {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : coneNonflatCert H (L + 1) Q
declarations.docstring: **Depth monotonicity, one bit**: a firing certificate keeps firing one depth deeper (floors held). Chain: cert(L) ⟹ no common point at `L` (argmin) ⟹ no common point at `L+1` (halving) ⟹ cert(L+1) (completeness).

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_mono_of_le
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_mono_of_le {H L L' : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : coneNonflatCert H L' Q
declarations.docstring: **Depth monotonicity**: a firing certificate fires at EVERY deeper depth (floors held at the base). `nonflat_L_min` is therefore THE firing threshold: below it silence, at and above it certification. In any supply statement `∃L` may be replaced by any canonical large-enough depth.

declarations.name: coneCommonPoint_subset
declarations.signature: theorem coneCommonPoint_subset {H L : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (h : coneCommonPoint H L Q') : coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: A common point of a menu restricts to a common point of any sub-menu: the same witness satisfies fewer arc constraints.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_supermenu
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_supermenu {H L : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : coneNonflatCert H L Q'
declarations.docstring: **Menu monotonicity, same depth, no floor**: a firing certificate keeps firing for every supermenu at the SAME depth. If the sub-menu arcs already have empty intersection, adding arcs cannot refill it.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_seed_lift
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_seed_lift {H L L' : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : coneNonflatCert H L' Q'
declarations.docstring: **The seed-lift theorem**: a small seed menu `Q` firing at its own floor `L` inflates to every supermenu `Q'` at every deeper depth `L'`. Depth monotonicity carries the seed up to `L'`; menu monotonicity carries it out to `Q'`. Consequence for supply: it suffices that SOME prefix seed fires at its floor for arbitrarily large `t` — the full menu at the full depth follows for free, at no extra arithmetic cost.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_cells
declarations.docstring: First firing menus: the non-flatness certificate fires at `(t, Q, L) = (1, [1,2,3], 5)`, `(2, [1,2,3,4], 5)`, `(3, [1,2,3], 6)` — the probe-tabulated cells with the smallest totient arguments (all one-sided floors hold).

declarations.name: cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell : coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 8 [1, 3] ∧ (∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 2) (1 * periodLcm 2) L) ∧ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 2) (1 * periodLcm 2) 9
declarations.docstring: **The measured strict win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[1, 3]` refutes joint flatness at depth `8`, while NO pairwise certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8` — the pair first kills at depth `9`. The one-sided menu band is strictly sharper than the two-sided pairwise band (probe v3: 30 strict wins among the named menus, 169 in the exhaustive search).

declarations.name: cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell : coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3, 4] ∧ (¬ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3]) ∧ (¬ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 4]) ∧ (¬ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [3, 4]) ∧ (¬ certifiedKill (1 * periodLcm 2) (2 * periodLcm 2) 5) ∧ (¬ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 2) (2 * periodLcm 2) 5) ∧ (¬ certifiedKill (1 * periodLcm 2) (3 * periodLcm 2) 5)
declarations.docstring: **The measured joint win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[2, 3, 4]` refutes joint flatness at depth `5` while EVERY 2-vertex submenu refuter is silent at depth `5` and every pairwise certificate fails there too. The refutation is genuinely joint: the menu is inconsistent at a depth where every pair alone stays consistent. (Probe v3 tabulates 52 such joint cells over `t ≤ 7`, menus `⊆ {1..8}`; the named-menu census cell at `t = 8`, menu `[1,2,3,4]`, fires at depth 12 — its one-sided floor, slack ZERO — with the same joint silence.)

declarations.name: le_periodLcm
declarations.signature: lemma le_periodLcm (t : ℕ) : t ≤ periodLcm t

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Endpoint-certificate variant of the wave-22 one-parameter collapse: certified window kills along `lcm(1..t)` — arbitrarily large `t`, arbitrarily large `N` — prove #249. (Wave 22 landed only the survivor form.)

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-23 diagonal collapse.** #249 follows from a SINGLE ℕ-indexed sequence of decidable statements: the endpoint certificate firing on the diagonal `(h, N) = (periodLcm t, periodLcm t)` for infinitely many `t`. Rationality would supply a primitive period `h₀` and pre-period `N₀`; any `t ≥ max(h₀, N₀)` gives `h₀ ∣ periodLcm t` and `periodLcm t ≥ t ≥ N₀` simultaneously, so a diagonal kill at such `t` is a contradiction. No second parameter remains.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_survivor_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_survivor_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ K, survivorKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Survivor variant of the diagonal collapse.

declarations.name: eq_prime_pow_of_not_dvd_periodLcm
declarations.signature: theorem eq_prime_pow_of_not_dvd_periodLcm {t j : ℕ} (hj : 0 < j) (hlt : j < 2 * t) (hnd : ¬ j ∣ periodLcm t) : ∃ p k : ℕ, Nat.Prime p ∧ j = p ^ k ∧ t < j
declarations.docstring: **Window-structure theorem for the lcm ray.** Below `2t`, the only non-divisors of `lcm(1..t)` are bare prime powers exceeding `t`: every other window index divides the period. (Indices `j ≤ t` divide outright by `dvd_periodLcm`; this pins down the sparse exceptional set above `t`.)

declarations.name: coprime_ray_cofactor
declarations.signature: lemma coprime_ray_cofactor {j a : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hcof : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ a) : Nat.Coprime j (q * a + 1)
declarations.docstring: If every prime of `j` divides `a`, then `j` and `q·a + 1` are coprime: the cofactor is forced `≡ 1` mod every prime of `j`.

declarations.name: totient_ray_split
declarations.signature: theorem totient_ray_split {j a : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hcof : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ a) : Nat.totient (j * (q * a + 1)) = Nat.totient j * Nat.totient (q * a + 1)
declarations.docstring: Totient splits across the ray product `j·(q·a + 1)` whenever every prime of `j` divides `a`.

declarations.name: totient_periodLcm_ray_split
declarations.signature: theorem totient_periodLcm_ray_split {t j : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hdvd : j ∣ periodLcm t) (hclean : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ (periodLcm t / j)) : Nat.totient (q * periodLcm t + j) = Nat.totient j * Nat.totient (q * (periodLcm t / j) + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Lcm-ray window factorisation.** For a clean divisor `j` of `H = periodLcm t` — every prime of `j` still divides `H/j` — the window totient at `q·H + j` splits into the KNOWN local part `φ(j)` and the cofactor `φ(q·(H/j) + 1)`, whose argument avoids every prime of `j`'s saturation. This is the exact identity behind the wave-23 shadow decomposition: on the lcm ray the window is structured arithmetic, not totient noise.

declarations.name: diagonalKillDepth
declarations.signature: def diagonalKillDepth : ℕ → ℕ | 1 => 6 | 2 => 5 | 3 => 7 | 4 => 7 | 5 => 9 | 6 => 9 | 7 => 14 | 8 => 15 | _ => 0 /-- **Diagonal deposit.** The wave-23 predicate `P t` holds for every `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`: the endpoint certificate fires at `(H_t, H_t)` with the tabulated depth. Totient arguments stay ≤ 130 — a light kernel `decide`. -/
declarations.docstring: Certified diagonal kill depths for `1 ≤ t ≤ 8`, matching the wave-23 probe (`lcm_diagonal_shadow_probe.json`).

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six : ∀ t ∈ Finset.Icc 1 6, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalKillDepth t)
declarations.docstring: **Diagonal deposit.** The wave-23 predicate `P t` holds for every `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`: the endpoint certificate fires at `(H_t, H_t)` with the tabulated depth. Totient arguments stay ≤ 130 — a light kernel `decide`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_seven
declarations.docstring: `P 7`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..7) = 420`, depth `14`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_eight
declarations.docstring: `P 8`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..8) = 840`, depth `15`.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal (t : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ t) (h6 : t ≤ 6) : totientTail (periodLcm t + periodLcm t) - totientTail (periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: No lcm-diagonal tail difference is an integer at the first six scales: `D_{H_t}(H_t) ∉ ℤ` for `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`.

declarations.name: summable_lambert_pair
declarations.signature: theorem summable_lambert_pair (w v : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) (hv : ∀ m : ℕ, 0 < m → |v m| ≤ (m : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ+ × ℕ+ => w (p.1 : ℕ) * v (p.2 : ℕ) * r ^ ((p.1 : ℕ) * (p.2 : ℕ)))
declarations.docstring: Summability of the product-weighted geometric family over `ℕ+ × ℕ+` for weights of at most linear growth. The majorant is AM–GM: `|w(d)·v(m)| ≤ d·m ≤ d² + m²`, and each square rides Mathlib's `summable_prod_mul_pow` (once directly, once through `Prod.swap`).

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_pair_regroup
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_pair_regroup (w v : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) (hv : ∀ m : ℕ, 0 < m → |v m| ≤ (m : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' p : ℕ+ × ℕ+, w (p.1 : ℕ) * v (p.2 : ℕ) * r ^ ((p.1 : ℕ) * (p.2 : ℕ)) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e * v ((n : ℕ) / e)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed weighted product-to-divisor-sum rearrangement** with product weight `w(d)·v(m)`, both of at most linear growth on positive arguments. The double series over `ℕ+ × ℕ+` regroups along divisor antidiagonals into `∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e)·v(n/e))·rⁿ` — the Dirichlet convolution appears as the coefficient. Signed analogue of the kernel's `[0,1]`-weight identity.

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_linear_weight
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_linear_weight (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed linear-growth Lambert series identity**: `∑_d w(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e))·rⁿ` for `|w(d)| ≤ d`.

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ)) ^ 2) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed linear-growth squared-Lambert identity**: `∑_d w(d)·r^d/(1-r^d)² = ∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e)·(n/e))·rⁿ` for `|w(d)| ≤ d`. The inner geometric weight `m` is the derivative structure that makes the divisor coefficient a genuine Dirichlet convolution against `Id`.

declarations.name: totientZ
declarations.docstring: The Euler totient as an integer-valued arithmetic function.

declarations.name: totientZ_apply
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem totientZ_apply (n : ℕ) : totientZ n = (Nat.totient n : ℤ)

declarations.name: primWeight
declarations.docstring: **The primitive-conductor weight** `A = φ * μ` (Dirichlet convolution). `A(n)` counts the primitive Dirichlet characters of conductor `n` (OEIS A007431); it is multiplicative, nonnegative, vanishes exactly on `n ≡ 2 (mod 4)`, and satisfies `A(p) = p - 2`, `A(p^e) = (p-1)²·p^(e-2)`.

declarations.name: primWeight_mul_zeta
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_mul_zeta : primWeight * (↑(zeta : ArithmeticFunction ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) = totientZ
declarations.docstring: `A * ζ = φ`: summing the primitive-conductor weight over divisors recovers the totient — the inversion that makes `L(A) = S`.

declarations.name: sum_divisors_primWeight
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_primWeight (n : ℕ) : ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, primWeight e = (Nat.totient n : ℤ)

declarations.name: primWeight_apply_prime_pow
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_apply_prime_pow {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) {k : ℕ} (hk : k ≠ 0) : primWeight (p ^ k) = (Nat.totient (p ^ k) : ℤ) - (Nat.totient (p ^ (k - 1)) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Prime-power values: `A(p^k) = φ(p^k) - φ(p^(k-1))`. Only the divisor pairs `(p^k, 1)` and `(p^(k-1), p)` survive the Möbius factor.

declarations.name: primWeight_apply_prime
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_apply_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : primWeight p = (p : ℤ) - 2

declarations.name: primWeight_two
declarations.docstring: The weight vanishes at `2` — primitive characters mod 2 do not exist. This is the single local zero: `A(n) = 0` iff `n ≡ 2 (mod 4)`.

declarations.name: primWeight_nonneg
declarations.docstring: **Nonnegativity of the primitive-conductor weight.** Each prime-power factor is `φ(p^k) - φ(p^(k-1)) ≥ 0` since `φ` respects divisibility.

declarations.name: primWeight_le_totient
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_le_totient (n : ℕ) : primWeight n ≤ (Nat.totient n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Upper growth: `A(n) ≤ φ(n)` (immediate from `A ≥ 0` and `A * ζ = φ`).

declarations.name: abs_moebius_le_one
declarations.docstring: Upper growth: `A(n) ≤ φ(n)` (immediate from `A ≥ 0` and `A * ζ = φ`). -/ theorem primWeight_le_totient (n : ℕ) : primWeight n ≤ (Nat.totient n : ℤ) := by rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with rfl | hn · simp · calc primWeight n ≤ ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, primWeight e := Finset.single_le_sum (fun e _ => primWeight_nonneg e) (Nat.mem_divisors_self n hn.ne') _ = (Nat.totient n : ℤ) := sum_divisors_primWeight n /-! ## Möbius divisor sums

declarations.name: sum_divisors_moebius
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_moebius (n : ℕ) : ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, moebius e = if n = 1 then 1 else 0

declarations.name: sum_divisors_moebius_mul_div
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_moebius_mul_div (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, moebius e * ((n / e : ℕ) : ℤ) = (Nat.totient n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Möbius inversion of `∑_{d∣n} φ(d) = n`: the totient as the `μ * Id` convolution, in exactly the divisor-sum shape the squared Lambert engine emits.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_lambert
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_lambert {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **`L(φ)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑ φ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑ n·rⁿ`. The totient Lambert series collapses to the rational function `r/(1-r)²`.

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_lambert
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_lambert {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = r
declarations.docstring: **`L(μ)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑ μ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = r`. Only the `n = 1` divisor sum survives (`μ * ζ = ε`).

declarations.name: tsum_primWeight_lambert
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_primWeight_lambert {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((primWeight (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The positive lift, Lambert form**: `∑ A(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ`. The `#249` power series IS the Mersenne–Lambert transform of the nonnegative primitive-conductor weight (`A * ζ = φ`).

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_lambert_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_lambert_sq {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ)) ^ 2) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens, Lambert form**: `∑ μ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d)² = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ`. The `#249` power series as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series (`μ * Id = φ`).

declarations.name: two_pow_sub_one_pos
declarations.signature: private lemma two_pow_sub_one_pos (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (0 : ℝ) < (2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1
declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens, Lambert form**: `∑ μ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d)² = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ`. The `#249` power series as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series (`μ * Id = φ`). -/ theorem tsum_moebius_lambert_sq {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ)) ^ 2) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ) := by rw [tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq (fun e => ((moebius e : ℤ) : ℝ)) (fun d hd => by show |((moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ)| ≤ (d : ℝ) have h1 : |((moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ)| ≤ 1 := by rw [← Int.cast_abs] exact_mod_cast abs_moebius_le_one d exact h1.trans (by exact_mod_cast hd)) hr0 hr1] refine tsum_congr fun n => ?_ congr 1 have h := congrArg (fun z : ℤ => (z : ℝ)) (sum_divisors_moebius_mul_div (n : ℕ) n.pos) push_cast at h simpa using h /-! ## Evaluations at `r = 1/2`: the Mersenne forms

declarations.name: half_pow_term
declarations.signature: private lemma half_pow_term (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d) = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1)

declarations.name: half_pow_sq_term
declarations.signature: private lemma half_pow_sq_term (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d) ^ 2 = (2 : ℝ) ^ d / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2

declarations.name: mersenne_split
declarations.signature: private lemma mersenne_split (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (2 : ℝ) ^ d / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2 = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) + 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2

declarations.name: tsum_pnat_mul_half_pow
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pnat_mul_half_pow : ∑' n : ℕ+, ((n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ) = 2
declarations.docstring: `∑_{n≥1} n/2ⁿ = 2` — the rational value the totient Lambert series hits.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(φ) = 2`**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2^d - 1) = 2`, exactly. The Lambert transform of the `#249` coefficients at the `#249` point is rational — the entire difficulty of #249 lives on the power-series side.

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_half
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_half : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 1 / 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(μ) = 1/2`**: `∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d - 1) = 1/2`, exactly.

declarations.name: tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((primWeight (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The positive lift at `1/2`**: `∑_{d≥1} A(d)/(2^d - 1) = ∑_{n≥1} φ(n)·(1/2)ⁿ`. The `#249` constant IS a positive Erdős–Borwein-type constant: same shape as `E = ∑ 1/(2^d - 1)` (irrational, machine-checked in the kernel), with the weight `1` replaced by the primitive-conductor count `A ≥ 0`.

declarations.name: summable_moebius_div_mersenne
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_moebius_div_mersenne : Summable (fun d : ℕ+ => ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1))

declarations.name: summable_moebius_div_mersenne_sq
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_moebius_div_mersenne_sq : Summable (fun d : ℕ+ => ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2)

declarations.name: tsum_totient_half_pow_eq_half_add_moebius_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_half_pow_eq_half_add_moebius_sq : ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **The Möbius-square lens at `1/2`**: `∑ φ(n)·(1/2)ⁿ = 1/2 + ∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d - 1)²`.

declarations.name: doublingResidue
declarations.signature: def doublingResidue (p v n : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Least nonnegative residue of `p*2^n` modulo `v`. Under the coprimality hypotheses used below it is automatically the least *positive* residue.

declarations.name: doublingWrapDigit
declarations.signature: def doublingWrapDigit (p v n : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The quotient expelled when a least residue is doubled. Since the residue is below `v`, this quotient is always zero or one.

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount
declarations.signature: def doublingWrapCount (p v h : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Number of binary wraps in the first `h` transitions.

declarations.name: IsDoublingPeriod
declarations.docstring: The period hypothesis, separated from minimality: `h>0` and `2^h = 1 (mod v)`. Substituting the multiplicative order supplies this.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_lt
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_lt (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (n : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v n < v

declarations.name: doublingWrapDigit_le_one
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapDigit_le_one (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (n : ℕ) : doublingWrapDigit p v n ≤ 1

declarations.name: doublingWrapDigit_eq_zero_or_one
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapDigit_eq_zero_or_one (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (n : ℕ) : doublingWrapDigit p v n = 0 ∨ doublingWrapDigit p v n = 1

declarations.name: doublingResidue_pos
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_pos (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (j : ℕ) : 0 < doublingResidue p v j
declarations.docstring: For an odd modulus and reduced numerator, every canonical residue is strictly positive.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_succ
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_succ (p v n : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v (n + 1) = (2 * doublingResidue p v n) % v
declarations.docstring: Doubling and reducing advances the canonical residue cycle by one.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_carry
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_carry (p v n : ℕ) : 2 * doublingResidue p v n = v * doublingWrapDigit p v n + doublingResidue p v (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact binary carry recurrence for canonical residues.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_add_period
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_add_period (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (n : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v (n + h) = doublingResidue p v n
declarations.docstring: A doubling period closes every numerator residue cycle.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_add
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_add (p v N j : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v (N + j) = doublingResidue (p * 2 ^ N) v j
declarations.docstring: Shifting the tail index multiplies the starting numerator by the same power of two.

declarations.name: sum_range_shift_one
declarations.signature: theorem sum_range_shift_one (r : ℕ → ℕ) (h : ℕ) : (∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r (j + 1)) + r 0 = (∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r j) + r h
declarations.docstring: Summing a shifted finite sequence differs only by its two endpoints.

declarations.name: sum_range_add_eq_sum_range_of_periodic
declarations.signature: theorem sum_range_add_eq_sum_range_of_periodic (r : ℕ → ℕ) (h : ℕ) (hperiod : ∀ N : ℕ, r (N + h) = r N) : ∀ N : ℕ, ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r (N + j) = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r j
declarations.docstring: Every complete block of a periodic natural sequence has the same sum.

declarations.name: sum_eq_modulus_mul_wrapCount_of_closed_cycle
declarations.signature: theorem sum_eq_modulus_mul_wrapCount_of_closed_cycle (v h : ℕ) (r k : ℕ → ℕ) (hcarry : ∀ j : ℕ, 2 * r j = v * k j + r (j + 1)) (hclosed : r h = r 0) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r j = v * ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, k j
declarations.docstring: **Wrap identity for a closed binary residue cycle.**

declarations.name: sum_doublingResidue_eq_mul_wrapCount
declarations.signature: theorem sum_doublingResidue_eq_mul_wrapCount (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, doublingResidue p v j = v * doublingWrapCount p v h
declarations.docstring: Canonical form of the wrap identity.

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount_pos
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapCount_pos (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) : 0 < doublingWrapCount p v h
declarations.docstring: A nonempty positive closed residue cycle has at least one wrap.

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount_mul_pow_eq
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapCount_mul_pow_eq (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (N : ℕ) : doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h = doublingWrapCount p v h
declarations.docstring: The wrap count is invariant under rotation of a complete residue cycle.

declarations.name: exists_unique_wrap_of_sum_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem exists_unique_wrap_of_sum_eq_one (h : ℕ) (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hbit : ∀ j < h, wrap j ≤ 1) (hone : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, wrap j = 1) : ∃ t < h, wrap t = 1 ∧ ∀ j < h, j ≠ t → wrap j = 0
declarations.docstring: A bit-valued family whose sum is one has one and only one nonzero position in the displayed range.

declarations.name: accumulatedWrap
declarations.signature: def accumulatedWrap (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ | 0 => 0 | n + 1 => 2 * accumulatedWrap wrap n + wrap n /-- Unroll the binary carry recurrence through a finite prefix. -/ theorem recurrence_unroll (v n : ℕ) (r wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hrec : ∀ j < n, 2 * r j = v * wrap j + r (j + 1)) : 2 ^ n * r 0 = r n + v * accumulatedWrap wrap n
declarations.docstring: Binary value represented by a prefix of wrap digits, with the most recent digit least significant in `E(n+1)=2E(n)+wrap(n)`.

declarations.name: recurrence_unroll
declarations.signature: theorem recurrence_unroll (v n : ℕ) (r wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hrec : ∀ j < n, 2 * r j = v * wrap j + r (j + 1)) : 2 ^ n * r 0 = r n + v * accumulatedWrap wrap n
declarations.docstring: Unroll the binary carry recurrence through a finite prefix.

declarations.name: accumulatedWrap_eq_zero_of_zero
declarations.signature: private theorem accumulatedWrap_eq_zero_of_zero (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (n : ℕ) (hzero : ∀ j < n, wrap j = 0) : accumulatedWrap wrap n = 0

declarations.name: accumulatedWrap_eq_pow_of_unique
declarations.signature: theorem accumulatedWrap_eq_pow_of_unique (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) {h t : ℕ} (ht : t < h) (htone : wrap t = 1) (hother : ∀ j < h, j ≠ t → wrap j = 0) : accumulatedWrap wrap h = 2 ^ (h - 1 - t)

declarations.name: two_pow_sub_one_mod_two
declarations.signature: private theorem two_pow_sub_one_mod_two {h : ℕ} (hh : 0 < h) : (2 ^ h - 1) % 2 = 1

declarations.name: one_wrap_cycle_classification
declarations.signature: theorem one_wrap_cycle_classification (v h : ℕ) (r wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hh : 0 < h) (hrcop : (r 0).Coprime v) (hclosed : r h = r 0) (hrec : ∀ j < h, 2 * r j = v * wrap j + r (j + 1)) (hbit : ∀ j < h, wrap j ≤ 1) (hone : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, wrap j = 1) : v = 2 ^ h - 1 ∧ ∃ a < h, r 0 = 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: Full algebraic classification of a one-wrap closed cycle with reduced starting residue: the modulus is Mersenne and the start is a power of two.

declarations.name: one_doublingWrap_classification
declarations.signature: theorem one_doublingWrap_classification (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (_hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (hone : doublingWrapCount p v h = 1) : v = 2 ^ h - 1 ∧ ∃ a < h, doublingResidue p v 0 = 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: Canonical one-wrap theorem: the odd denominator is `2^h-1`, and the numerator residue lies in the power-of-two orbit.

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder
declarations.signature: noncomputable def oddDoublingOrder (v : ℕ) (hvodd : Odd v) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Mathlib realization of `ord_v(2)` for an odd modulus.

declarations.name: pow_oddDoublingOrder_mod_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem pow_oddDoublingOrder_mod_eq_one {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) : 2 ^ oddDoublingOrder v hvodd % v = 1

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder_pos
declarations.signature: theorem oddDoublingOrder_pos {v : ℕ} (hvodd : Odd v) : 0 < oddDoublingOrder v hvodd

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder_isPeriod
declarations.signature: theorem oddDoublingOrder_isPeriod {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) : IsDoublingPeriod v (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd)
declarations.docstring: The actual multiplicative order closes the canonical residue cycle.

declarations.name: oddOrder_doublingWrapCount_pos
declarations.signature: theorem oddOrder_doublingWrapCount_pos (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) : 0 < doublingWrapCount p v (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd)

declarations.name: one_oddOrder_doublingWrap_classification
declarations.signature: theorem one_oddOrder_doublingWrap_classification (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hone : doublingWrapCount p v (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd) = 1) : v = 2 ^ oddDoublingOrder v hvodd - 1 ∧ ∃ a < oddDoublingOrder v hvodd, doublingResidue p v 0 = 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: One-wrap classification instantiated at the actual multiplicative order.

declarations.name: IsOddSupportTailState
declarations.signature: def IsOddSupportTailState (A : Set ℕ) (p v : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A natural state which is simultaneously the odd-denominator scaling of the true support tail and, modulo `v`, the canonical doubling residue of a numerator. The rational-fraction bridge below constructs this state after removing the power of two from a displayed denominator.

declarations.name: IsShiftedOddSupportTailState
declarations.signature: def IsShiftedOddSupportTailState (A : Set ℕ) (p v c : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Odd tail state after removing the power-of-two part of a displayed denominator.

declarations.name: IsOddSupportTailState.shifted_zero
declarations.signature: private theorem IsOddSupportTailState.shifted_zero {A : Set ℕ} {p v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℕ} (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) : IsShiftedOddSupportTailState A p v 0 u
declarations.docstring: The unshifted state is exactly the shifted state at `c = 0`.

declarations.name: oddTailExcess
declarations.signature: def oddTailExcess (u : ℕ → ℕ) (v N : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Integral excess of an odd tail state above its least residue.

declarations.name: shiftedOddSupportTail_eq_residue_div_add_excess
declarations.signature: theorem shiftedOddSupportTail_eq_residue_div_add_excess (A : Set ℕ) (p c : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsShiftedOddSupportTailState A p v c u) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + N) = (doublingResidue p v N : ℝ) / (v : ℝ) + (oddTailExcess u v N : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact pointwise decomposition of a genuine shifted odd tail state into its least residue and a nonnegative integral excess.

declarations.name: oddSupportTail_eq_residue_div_add_excess
declarations.signature: theorem oddSupportTail_eq_residue_div_add_excess (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N = (doublingResidue p v N : ℝ) / (v : ℝ) + (oddTailExcess u v N : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact pointwise decomposition of a genuine odd tail state into its least residue and a nonnegative integral excess.

declarations.name: sum_supportTail_block_eq_wrapCount_add_excess
declarations.signature: theorem sum_supportTail_block_eq_wrapCount_add_excess (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (N : ℕ) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (N + j) = (doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) + ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, (oddTailExcess u v (N + j) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact block identity: a complete residue cycle contributes its wrap count, and every additional lift of a tail state contributes one nonnegative integer. This is the finite algebra behind both the order lower bound and the exact excess-mean identity.

declarations.name: wrapCount_le_sum_supportTail_block
declarations.signature: theorem wrapCount_le_sum_supportTail_block (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (N : ℕ) : (doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (N + j)
declarations.docstring: Dropping the nonnegative excess terms gives the order-sensitive local block lower bound.

declarations.name: numerator_pos_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem numerator_pos_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : 0 < p
declarations.docstring: A nonempty positive support forces the numerator of every displayed positive-denominator fraction to be positive.

declarations.name: exists_shifted_odd_tail_states_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_shifted_odd_tail_states_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) = 2 * u n - (v : ℤ) * (supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) : ℤ)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ (2 : ℤ) ^ n * p [ZMOD (v : ℤ)])
declarations.docstring: Removing the power-of-two part of a displayed denominator gives a positive integer state equal to `v` times the shifted true tail. The state also carries the exact additive recurrence and its numerator residue class.

declarations.name: exists_shifted_odd_tail_nat_states_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_shifted_odd_tail_nat_states_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℕ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p.toNat * 2 ^ n [MOD v])
declarations.docstring: Natural-number form of the shifted odd tail state.

declarations.name: wrapCount_le_sum_tail_of_modEq
declarations.signature: theorem wrapCount_le_sum_tail_of_modEq (coeff : ℕ → ℕ) (shift p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (htail : ∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail coeff (shift + n)) (hmod : ∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p * 2 ^ n [MOD v]) (N : ℕ) : (doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail coeff (shift + (N + j))
declarations.docstring: Any shifted natural tail state with the correct numerator congruence inherits the canonical wrap-count lower bound on every complete block.

declarations.name: support_fraction_wrapCount_le_tail_block
declarations.signature: theorem support_fraction_wrapCount_le_tail_block (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (N : ℕ) : (doublingWrapCount (p.toNat * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + (N + j))
declarations.docstring: Fraction-facing local order-wrap theorem. The only normalization here is the explicit denominator `2^c*v`; reducedness and oddness enter the order corollary below.

declarations.name: reciprocalSupportTerm
declarations.signature: noncomputable def reciprocalSupportTerm (A : Set ℕ) (a : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The reciprocal summand of a support, with exponent zero harmlessly normalized to zero by real division.

declarations.name: reciprocalMass
declarations.signature: noncomputable def reciprocalMass (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The reciprocal mass `ρ(A) = ∑_{a∈A} 1/a`.

declarations.name: ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast
declarations.signature: def ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A : Set ℕ) (r : ℝ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Extended-real-free statement of a reciprocal-mass lower bound: either the reciprocal family diverges, in which case every finite lower bound is automatic, or its real tsum is at least `r`.

declarations.name: divisorFrequency
declarations.signature: noncomputable def divisorFrequency (A : Set ℕ) (N a : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The finite divisor frequency of `a` in the first `N` positive integers.

declarations.name: sum_supportCoeff_range_eq_sum_div
declarations.signature: theorem sum_supportCoeff_range_eq_sum_div (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, supportCoeff A (n + 1) = ∑ a ∈ Finset.range (N + 1), Set.indicator A (fun a : ℕ => N / a) a
declarations.docstring: Exact finite double counting: summing the support coefficient through `N` is the sum of the counts `⌊N/a⌋` over the support.

declarations.name: tendsto_nat_div_frequency
